Issue to solve: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808
Your prepared branch: issue-808-bf5af32dbcdd
Your prepared working directory: /tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784535043401
Your prepared Pull Request: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/809

Download all logs and collect data related about the issue to this repository, and compile that data into the ./dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809 folder.

Use the collected evidence to do a deep analysis (search online for additional facts and data), reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events, list each and every requirement from the issue, find the root cause of each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement. Also check online for known existing components/libraries that solve a similar problem or can help.

If there is not enough data to find the actual root cause, add debug output and a verbose mode (if not already present) so the root cause can be found on the next iteration. Keep the default state switched off.

If the issue is related to another repository/project, report issues on GitHub for that project when possible. Each report must contain reproducible examples, workarounds, and suggestions for fixing the issue in code.

Double-check that the requirements are fully applied to the entire codebase: if an issue exists in multiple places, apply it in all of them.

Proceed.

message.content:
Issue to solve: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808
Your prepared branch: issue-808-bf5af32dbcdd
Your prepared working directory: /tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784535043401
Your prepared Pull Request: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/809

Download all logs and collect data related about the issue to this repository, and compile that data into the ./dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809 folder.

Use the collected evidence to do a deep analysis (search online for additional facts and data), reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events, list each and every requirement from the issue, find the root cause of each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement. Also check online for known existing components/libraries that solve a similar problem or can help.

If there is not enough data to find the actual root cause, add debug output and a verbose mode (if not already present) so the root cause can be found on the next iteration. Keep the default state switched off.

If the issue is related to another repository/project, report issues on GitHub for that project when possible. Each report must contain reproducible examples, workarounds, and suggestions for fixing the issue in code.

Double-check that the requirements are fully applied to the entire codebase: if an issue exists in multiple places, apply it in all of them.

Proceed.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by gathering the issue data.

input.command: gh issue view 808 && echo "=== COMMENTS ===" && gh api repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808/comments --paginate && echo "=== PR ===" && gh pr view 809

        working-directory: desktop
        env:
          CSC_LINK: ${{ secrets.MAC_CSC_LINK }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.MAC_CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY }}

          APPLE_API_ISSUER: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_ISSUER }}
          DEBUG: electron-builder,electron-notarize*
        run: npx --no-install electron-builder ${{ matrix.ebflag }} --publish never

      # Issue #479: electron-builder 26 changed macOS signing control flow. Its
      # MacPackager.sign() now calls findSigningIdentity(), which returns null --
      # skipping signing entirely and NEVER invoking the custom `mac.sign` hook --
      # unless a real identity is found OR `mac.identity` is exactly "-" (ad-hoc).
      # See packages/app-builder-lib/src/targets/mac/MacTargetHelper.ts: only the
      # `qualifier === "-"` branch yields an Identity when no certificate exists.
      # Without `-c.mac.identity=-` the produced .app had no _CodeSignature, so the
      # pre-upload smoke test failed with "missing its signed CodeResources
      # envelope" and macOS assets were never published. Passing identity="-" makes
      # electron-builder reach doSign(), which runs our ad-hoc hook (deep ad-hoc
      # sign via @electron/osx-sign + an explicit root-bundle re-seal).
      - name: Package desktop app (macOS ad-hoc)
        if: matrix.ebflag == '--mac' && steps.mac_secrets.outputs.has_secrets != 'true'
        working-directory: desktop
        env:
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: "false"
          MACOS_ADHOC_SIGN: "1"
          FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG: "1"
          DEBUG: electron-builder,electron-osx-sign*
        run: npx --no-install electron-builder ${{ matrix.ebflag }} --publish never -c.mac.notarize=false -c.mac.identity=- -c.mac.sign=./scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs

      - name: Normalize desktop artifact names
        working-directory: desktop
        run: node scripts/normalize-artifacts.mjs

      - name: Smoke test macOS release artifacts

        working-directory: desktop
        env:
          MACOS_BUILD_MODE: ${{ steps.mac_secrets.outputs.mode }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          shopt -s nullglob

          VERSION="$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")"
          case "${{ matrix.label }}" in

<result># CI/CD Gap Analysis — `link-assistant/formal-ai` vs. link-foundation pipeline templates

Scope: `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784535043401/.github/**`, the rust/js/python `*-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` repos, and `link-assistant/hive-mind/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` (380 lines). No repo files were modified.

## 1. Inventory of `.github/` in formal-ai

`.github/` contains **only** `workflows/release.yml` (1625 lines) and `workflows/desktop-release.yml` (650 lines). There are **no** `.github/actions/*` composite actions, **no** `.github/dependabot.yml`, no issue/PR templates, no `CODEOWNERS`.

### `release.yml` — "CI/CD Pipeline"

Triggers (L3–L30): `push` → `main`; `pull_request` (opened/synchronize/reopened); `workflow_dispatch` (inputs `release_mode` ∈ {instant, changelog-pr}, `bump_type`, `description`).
Concurrency L36–L42: `cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' }}`. `permissions: contents: read` (L33).

| Job | Line | `if:` (condensed) | Runs on PR? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detect-changes` | 62 | `github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch'` | yes |
| `changelog` (fragment check) | 93 | `event == pull_request && any-code-changed` | **PR only** |
| `version-check` | 117 | `event == pull_request` | **PR only** |
| `lint` (fmt, clippy, `cargo metadata --locked`, `cargo doc` ×2, file-size, terminology, hardcoded-language) | 143 | `always() && !cancelled() && (push ‖ dispatch ‖ any-code/rs/toml/docs/workflow-changed)` | yes |
| `test` (matrix, ubuntu only) | 334 | same shape, minus `docs-changed` | yes |
| `coverage` | 396 | `push ‖ dispatch ‖ rs-changed ‖ toml-changed` | yes (if rs/toml) |
| `build` (cargo build --release, package, crate-size) | 470 | `needs.lint==success && needs.test==success` | yes |
| `auto-release` (publish crates.io, Docker GHCR+Hub, tag, GH release) | 508 | `event==push && ref==refs/heads/main && build success` | **no — main push only** |
| `manual-release` | 761 | `event==workflow_dispatch && release_mode=='instant'` | **no** |
| `changelog-pr` | 979 | `workflow_dispatch && release_mode=='changelog-pr'` | **no** |
| `test-e2e-local` | 1031 | `push ‖ dispatch ‖ any-code-changed` | yes |
| `test-agent-cli-e2e` | 1111 | `push ‖ dispatch ‖ any-code/rs/toml/workflow-changed` | yes |
| `deploy-pages` | 1431 | `build success && ((push&&main&&auto-release success) ‖ (dispatch&&instant&&manual-release success))` | **no** |
| `test-e2e-pages` | 1582 | `needs.deploy-pages.result == 'success'` | **no** |

### `desktop-release.yml`

Triggers (L31–L41): `workflow_dispatch(tag)`, `release: [published]`, `workflow_run: workflows:["CI/CD Pipeline"] types:[completed]`. Concurrency L53–54 (`cancel-in-progress: false`).
Jobs: `resolve` (L65, `if:` L81 — `event != workflow_run || (head_branch=='main' && conclusion not in {cancelled,skipped})`), `build` (L113, `needs.resolve.outputs.should_build=='true'`, Tauri desktop matrix), `vscode` (L451, same gate, packages `.vsix`), `finalize` (L532, `always() && should_build`). **None of these ever run on a pull_request.**

## 2. Template file trees

**rust template** — `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient/action.yml`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`; `scripts/`: bump-version, check-cargo-lock, check-changelog-fragment, check-crate-size, check-file-size, check-release-needed, check-version-modification, collect-changelog, create-changelog-fragment, create-github-release, detect-code-changes, get-bump-type, get-version, git-config, publish-crate, release-naming, rust-paths, smoke-test-published-crate, version-and-commit, wait-for-crate (all `.rs`).
Jobs: detect-changes(56) · changelog(86) · version-check(110) · **cargo-lock(137)** · lint(167, `needs:[detect-changes,cargo-lock]`) · test(219) · coverage(279) · build(335) · auto-release(373) · manual-release(554) · changelog-pr(712) · deploy-docs(771).

**js template** — `.github/actions/{publish-dockerhub,setup-buildx-resilient}/action.yml`, `.github/workflows/{release,example-app,links}.yml`; 30+ `scripts/*.mjs` incl. `simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, `check-file-line-limits.sh`, `check-web-archive.mjs`, `push-main-with-rebase-retry.sh`.
Jobs: detect-changes(53) · test-compilation(80) · **check-file-line-limits(97)** · version-check(128) · changeset-check(147) · lint(190, incl. **secretlint** L234) · test(240) · **validate-docs(333)** · release(377) · instant-release(482) · docker-publish(552) · changeset-pr(595). `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` is invoked in three jobs (L114, L210, L284).

**python template** — `.github/workflows/{docs,release}.yml`; `scripts/`: bump_version, check_file_size, create_github_release, create_manual_changeset, detect_code_changes, format_release_notes, publish_to_pypi, release_naming, smoke_test_published_package, validate_changeset, version_and_commit (all `.py`).

## 3. Best-practices checklist → status

| # | Practice (BEST-PRACTICES.md) | formal-ai | Evidence |

| 1 | `detect-changes` job gating all others | ✅ | `release.yml:62-91`, `scripts/detect-code-changes.rs` |
| 2 | File-size limit enforced in CI | ⚠️ present but buried in `lint` | `release.yml:223-225` (`scripts/check-file-size.rs`); templates run it as its own fast job (js `check-file-line-limits`, L97) |

## 4. Checks that CANNOT gate a PR (the core of issue #808)

Everything below runs **only** on push-to-main / release / `workflow_dispatch`, so a PR can merge while any of it is broken:

1. **Docker image build** — `docker/build-push-action@v7` at `release.yml:650`, `:710` (auto-release) and `:877`, `:936` (manual-release). A `Dockerfile` regression is invisible until after merge and after crates.io publish.
2. **`verify-docker-runtime.sh`** — only reachable from the release jobs.
3. **crates.io packaging/publish path** — `publish-crate.rs`, `wait-for-crate.rs`, `smoke-test-published-crate.sh`: only in `auto-release`/`manual-release` (L508/L761). (`build` L470 does run `cargo package --list` + crate-size on PRs, so partial coverage exists.)
4. **`deploy-pages`** (`release.yml:1431`) — Pages artifact assembly, `stamp-pages-artifact.sh`, `cargo doc` for `/docs/api`, `wait-for-pages-deployment.sh`. Never exercised pre-merge.
5. **`test-e2e-pages`** (`release.yml:1582`) — the entire GitHub-Pages Playwright suite is post-deploy only.
6. **`desktop-release.yml` `build`** (`desktop-release.yml:113`) — Tauri desktop builds for all OS targets, incl. macOS signing. A change to `src-tauri/**` or the signing steps is only discovered after a release tag exists.
7. **`desktop-release.yml` `vscode`** (`:451`) — `.vsix` packaging.
8. **`desktop-release.yml` `finalize`** (`:532`) — SHA256SUMS + provenance.
9. **`resolve` / `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh`** (`:65`) — release-resolution logic changes are untestable in a PR.
10. **`changelog-pr`** (`release.yml:979`) — dispatch-only.
11. **Version-bump machinery** — `version-and-commit.rs`, `get-bump-type.rs`, `collect-changelog.rs`, `create-github-release.rs`: executed only inside release jobs. (`version-and-commit-tests.rs` exists in `scripts/` but is not wired into any workflow step — worth confirming it runs under `cargo test`.)

Also note `coverage` (L401) omits `workflow-changed`, so a workflow-only PR skips it, and `test`/`coverage`/`lint` conditions each list a different subset of `detect-changes` outputs — drift risk.

## 5. Recommended minimal diffs

**(a) PR-gate the Docker build** — add to `release.yml`, before `auto-release`:

```yaml
  docker-pr-check:
    name: Docker Build Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    needs: [detect-changes]
    if: >-
      !cancelled() && github.event_name == 'pull_request' &&
      (needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' ||
       needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - run: bash scripts/free-runner-disk.sh
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with: { context: ., push: false, load: true, tags: formal-ai:pr, cache-from: type=gha, cache-to: type=gha,mode=max }
      - run: bash scripts/verify-docker-runtime.sh
```
Requires `docker-changed` be added to `detect-changes` outputs (`release.yml:67-71`) and to `scripts/detect-code-changes.rs`.

**(b) PR-gate desktop/VS Code builds** — add `pull_request:` to `desktop-release.yml:31` and a dry-run path:
```yaml
on:
  pull_request:
    paths: ['src-tauri/**', 'editors/**', '.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml', 'scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh']
```
plus in `resolve` (L81) `github.event_name == 'pull_request' ||` and in `build`/`vscode` `if: needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true' || github.event_name == 'pull_request'`, with all upload/sign/publish steps guarded by `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'`. Cheapest variant: restrict the PR matrix to `ubuntu-latest` only.

**(c) PR-gate the Pages artifact** — split `deploy-pages` (L1431) into `build-pages` (artifact assembly + `cargo doc`, runs on PR) and `deploy-pages` (`needs: build-pages`, keeps the current main/dispatch `if:`). Run `test-e2e-local` against the built artifact on PRs.

**(d) Fresh-merge validation** — port `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` from the js template (or reimplement in bash) and insert after `actions/checkout@v6` in `lint` (L166), `test` (L359) and `changelog` (L100):
```yaml
      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env: { BASE_REF: '${{ github.base_ref }}' }
        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh
```
Note this needs `fetch-depth: 0` — already set in `lint`/`changelog`, but `test` (L359) uses a bare `actions/checkout@v6`, so add `with: { fetch-depth: 0 }` there.

**(e) Secrets scan** — one step in `lint`:
```yaml
      - name: Secrets scan
        run: npx --yes -p secretlint -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend secretlint "**/*"
```

**(f) Fast-fail tier** — extract `check-file-size.rs` + `cargo metadata --locked` from `lint` into two ~20 s jobs (`check-file-line-limits`, `cargo-lock`, mirroring rust template L137) and make `lint`/`test`/`coverage` `needs:` them.

**(g) Cancellation semantics** — replace `always() && !cancelled() &&` with `!cancelled() &&` at L151, L343, L401, L475, L512, L767, L1035, L1115, L1437. The inline comments claim `always()` is needed because `detect-changes` is skipped on `workflow_dispatch`; that is true for `always()` vs. default, but `!cancelled()` alone already evaluates on skipped dependencies (actions/runner#491 applies to the default condition, not to `!cancelled()`), and `always()` is what breaks cancellation propagation.

**(h) Add `.github/dependabot.yml`** (cargo, npm for `tests/e2e`, github-actions ecosystems) — present in neither formal-ai nor the templates, but recommended.

## 6. Defects found in the TEMPLATES (report upstream)

**D1 — `link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`: no Docker/artifact PR gate.** `release.yml` builds and pushes Docker images only inside `auto-release` (L373) / `manual-release` (L554). Repro: create a PR breaking `Dockerfile`; the full pipeline is green, and the failure surfaces only after merge, *after* `cargo publish` has already run in the same job — leaving a published crate with no matching image. Workaround: the `docker-pr-check` job in §5(a). Fix: upstream should add that job and reuse `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient` with `push: false` on PRs.

**D2 — rust template is missing `simulate-fresh-merge`.** The js template ships `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` and calls it in three jobs (js `release.yml` L114/210/284); the rust template calls it nowhere, and BEST-PRACTICES §7 declares it mandatory. Repro: open PR A and PR B touching the same file; merge A; B's checks still validate the stale merge preview and pass. Workaround: add the step manually. Fix: port the script (it is language-agnostic bash) into the rust and python templates.

**D3 — rust and python templates have no secrets scan.** BEST-PRACTICES §11 requires one; only the js template has `secretlint` (L234). Fix: add the same `npx secretlint` step (or `trufflehog` for non-Node repos) to `lint` in both.

**D4 — python template lacks a `.github/actions/` directory and any Docker publishing path**, so `check-docker-publish`/`publish-dockerhub` equivalents are absent despite being documented as template features.

**D5 — BEST-PRACTICES.md §10 contradicts the templates.** The doc says "Use `!cancelled()` instead of `always()` so cancellation propagates," yet rust template `release.yml` L340 and the js/python equivalents use `always() && !cancelled() && …` throughout — which every downstream repo (including formal-ai, L151 etc.) has copied verbatim along with a comment asserting `always()` is required. Fix: either correct the templates to `!cancelled()` or amend the doc to explain the `workflow_dispatch`/skipped-dependency caveat; today the two disagree and downstream repos inherit the weaker semantics.</result>

2. **`verify-docker-runtime.sh`** — only reachable from the release jobs.
3. **crates.io packaging/publish path** — `publish-crate.rs`, `wait-for-crate.rs`, `smoke-test-published-crate.sh`: only in `auto-release`/`manual-release` (L508/L761). (`build` L470 does run `cargo package --list` + crate-size on PRs, so partial coverage exists.)
4. **`deploy-pages`** (`release.yml:1431`) — Pages artifact assembly, `stamp-pages-artifact.sh`, `cargo doc` for `/docs/api`, `wait-for-pages-deployment.sh`. Never exercised pre-merge.
5. **`test-e2e-pages`** (`release.yml:1582`) — the entire GitHub-Pages Playwright suite is post-deploy only.
6. **`desktop-release.yml` `build`** (`desktop-release.yml:113`) — Tauri desktop builds for all OS targets, incl. macOS signing. A change to `src-tauri/**` or the signing steps is only discovered after a release tag exists.

**(a) PR-gate the Docker build** — add to `release.yml`, before `auto-release`:

```yaml
  docker-pr-check:
    name: Docker Build Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    needs: [detect-changes]
    if: >-
      !cancelled() && github.event_name == 'pull_request' &&
      (needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' ||
       needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - run: bash scripts/free-runner-disk.sh
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with: { context: ., push: false, load: true, tags: formal-ai:pr, cache-from: type=gha, cache-to: type=gha,mode=max }
      - run: bash scripts/verify-docker-runtime.sh
```
Requires `docker-changed` be added to `detect-changes` outputs (`release.yml:67-71`) and to `scripts/detect-code-changes.rs`.

**(b) PR-gate desktop/VS Code builds** — add `pull_request:` to `desktop-release.yml:31` and a dry-run path:
```yaml
on:
  pull_request:
    paths: ['src-tauri/**', 'editors/**', '.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml', 'scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh']
```
plus in `resolve` (L81) `github.event_name == 'pull_request' ||` and in `build`/`vscode` `if: needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true' || github.event_name == 'pull_request'`, with all upload/sign/publish steps guarded by `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'`. **(c) PR-gate the Pages artifact** — split `deploy-pages` (L1431) into `build-pages` (artifact assembly + `cargo doc`, runs on PR) and `deploy-pages` (`needs: build-pages`, keeps the current main/dispatch `if:`). Run `test-e2e-local` against the built artifact on PRs.

**(d) Fresh-merge validation** — port `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` from the js template (or reimplement in bash) and insert after `actions/checkout@v6` in `lint` (L166), `test` (L359) and `changelog` (L100):
```yaml
      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env: { BASE_REF: '${{ github.base_ref }}' }
        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh
```
Note this needs `fetch-depth: 0` — already set in `lint`/`changelog`, but `test` (L359) uses a bare `actions/checkout@v6`, so add `with: { fetch-depth: 0 }` there.

**(e) Secrets scan** — one step in `lint`:
```yaml
      - name: Secrets scan
        run: npx --yes -p secretlint -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend secretlint "**/*"
```

**(f) Fast-fail tier** — extract `check-file-size.rs` + `cargo metadata --locked` from `lint` into two ~20 s jobs (`check-file-line-limits`, `cargo-lock`, mirroring rust template L137) and make `lint`/`test`/`coverage` `needs:` them.

**(h) Add `.github/dependabot.yml`** (cargo, npm for `tests/e2e`, github-actions ecosystems) — present in neither formal-ai nor the templates, but recommended.

**D1 — `link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`: no Docker/artifact PR gate.** `release.yml` builds and pushes Docker images only inside `auto-release` (L373) / `manual-release` (L554). Repro: create a PR breaking `Dockerfile`; the full pipeline is green, and the failure surfaces only after merge, *after* `cargo publish` has already run in the same job — leaving a published crate with no matching image. # === SELF-HOSTING EVIDENCE CHECK ===
  # Issue #808: run 29723467001 failed the whole Auto Release job with
  # "no committed Formal-AI-Evidence in 10e65ae2 records session
  # issue-804-claude-20260720". The invariant (CONTRIBUTING.md: every commit
  # carrying Formal-AI-Session must also carry a Formal-AI-Evidence file that
  # contains "formal-ai" and the exact session id) was only ever enforced at
  # release time, on main, after merge -- where nothing can be fixed without a
  # new PR. This job runs the *same* code path over the PR's own commits so a
  # violating commit is rejected before it can break a release.
  evidence-check:
    name: Self-Hosting Evidence Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Install rust-script
        run: bash scripts/install-rust-script.sh

      - name: Validate Formal-AI trailers on this pull request
        env:
          GITHUB_BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          base="origin/${GITHUB_BASE_REF}"
          merge_base="$(git merge-base "$base" HEAD)"
          echo "Validating commits in ${merge_base}..HEAD"
          rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --since "$merge_base" --until HEAD

  # === VERSION CHECK - prevents manual version modification in PRs ===

input.content:
# Issue #808 — CI/CD false positives, false negatives, warnings and errors

Session: `issue-808-claude-20260720`
Repository: formal-ai (`link-assistant/formal-ai`)
Pull request: [#809](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/809)
Evidence collected in: `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/`

## 1. Evidence collected

| File | Content |
| --- | --- |
| `ci-logs/run-29723467001-failed.log` | CI/CD Pipeline, `main` @ `76bdb6b5`, failed |
| `ci-logs/run-29724500254-failed.log` | Desktop Release, `main` @ `76bdb6b5`, failed |
| `ci-logs/run-29724500254-full.log` | Same run, complete log (used to prove an *absence* of output) |
| `ci-logs/run-29720321919-failed.log` | Desktop Release, `main` @ `8b5acee0`, failed (same signature) |
| `ci-logs/run-29719602956-failed.log` | CI/CD Pipeline, `main` @ `8b5acee0`, failed |
| `ci-logs/run-*.json` | Run/job metadata |

## 2. Timeline

| Time (UTC, 2026-07-20) | Event |
| --- | --- |
| 05:38 | CI/CD Pipeline run 29719602956 fails on `8b5acee0` |
| 05:56 | Desktop Release run 29720321919 fails on `8b5acee0` (macOS x64 + arm64) |
| 06:20–07:02 | Five Desktop Release runs conclude `skipped` (gated on `workflow_run` from a non-successful CI/CD Pipeline) |
| 07:04 | CI/CD Pipeline run 29723467001 fails on `76bdb6b5` — Auto Release, "Collect changelog and bump version" |
| 07:24 | Desktop Release run 29724500254 fails on `76bdb6b5` — identical macOS codesign failure |
| 07:51 | Desktop Release run 29725916384 concludes `skipped` |
| 08:10 | Issue comment: *"All checks must be in Pull Request stage, not only on main."* |

The `skipped` runs are **not** a defect: `desktop-release.yml` triggers on
`workflow_run` from "CI/CD Pipeline" and its `resolve` job correctly refuses to
build when the upstream run did not succeed. They are downstream symptoms of the
two real failures below.

## 3. Requirements extracted from the issue

| # | Requirement | Source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R1 | Fix every false positive, false negative, warning and error in CI/CD | issue body |
| R2 | Every check must run at the **pull request** stage, not only on `main`, so each PR can produce a working release | issue comment 5020083365 |
| R3 | Compare every workflow/CI file against the three `link-foundation/*-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` repos and adopt their best practices | issue body |
| R4 | Report upstream issues against the templates when the same defect exists there | issue body |
| R5 | Follow `link-assistant/hive-mind` `docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` | issue body |
| R6 | Collect logs and evidence into `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809` | task brief |

## 4. Failure A — macOS ad-hoc codesign

### Symptom

Both `Build macos-x64` and `Build macos-arm64` fail in *Package desktop app
(macOS ad-hoc)*:

```
.../formal-ai Desktop.app/Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/Frameworks/Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework:
  replacing existing signature
  unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework
⨯ Command failed: codesign --sign - --force --timestamp=none --options runtime \
  --entitlements build/entitlements.mac.plist .../Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework
```

`Publish SHA256SUMS.txt + provenance` then fails closed with
`No artifacts from: macos-x64 macos-arm64`. That second failure is **correct
behaviour** (added deliberately in `1b0ed3f8`, "stop desktop-release from
reporting partial builds as complete") — it is a true negative, not a defect.

### Root cause

`desktop/package.json` ships Playwright's Chrome-for-Testing tree as
`extraResources` → `Contents/Resources/browser-runtime`. That tree contains
`Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework`, whose layout is **not** a
sealed macOS framework bundle: files sit directly in the framework root rather
than under `Versions/A`. `codesign` refuses such a bundle with *"unsealed
contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework"*. It is signed
upstream by Google and must be treated as an opaque resource.

`desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` already contains an exclusion
(`isBundledBrowserRuntime`, added in `46aa1dd7`) and its unit tests pass
locally. It nevertheless did not take effect in CI.

Decisive observation from `ci-logs/run-29724500254-full.log`:

* the step's env block shows `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG: 1` (workflow line 232);
* electron-builder logs `• executing custom sign  file=release/mac/formal-ai Desktop.app`;
* yet `grep -c "\[adhoc-sign-mac\]"` over the **complete** run log returns `0`,
  and `grep -c "Skipped\.\.\."` (electron-osx-sign's log line for an ignored
  path) also returns `0`.

So the exclusion never ran, even though the hook was resolved. The failing
`codesign` invocation has electron-osx-sign's own argument order
(`--sign - --force …`), i.e. it comes from `signApplication()`'s child loop in
`@electron/osx-sign`, which consults `opts.ignore` — the very list the hook was
supposed to extend.

Two things were therefore wrong, and both are fixed:

1. **The exclusion depended on a single, unverified layer.** It lived only in
   the custom `mac.sign` hook. If the hook does not run (or runs with an
   `app` path that does not resolve as expected), nothing excludes the browser
   runtime. Fix: declare the exclusion in electron-builder configuration itself,
   as `build.mac.signIgnore`. `app-builder-lib`'s
   `MacTargetHelper.buildSignOptions()` compiles `signIgnore` entries into
   regexes and folds them into the `ignore` predicate handed to
   `@electron/osx-sign` — **independently of whether a custom sign hook is
   used**. Verified against the installed `app-builder-lib@26.15.3`
   (`out/mac/MacTargetHelper.js:53-107`, option declared at
   `out/options/macOptions.d.ts:163`).
2. **The diagnostics were unobservable.** The debug logger wrote to
   `process.stdout`; electron-builder's logger and electron-osx-sign's `debug`
   both write to `stderr`. Fix: route `[adhoc-sign-mac]` output to `stderr`, and
   emit exactly one unconditional line when the hook is entered, so the next run
   answers "did the hook execute at all?" without guesswork. The detailed
   per-file trace stays behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG`, **default off**.

### Related warning (electron-builder false positive)

```
• ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the
  com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement …
```

`desktop/build/entitlements.mac.plist` already grants
`com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`. electron-builder emits this
warning whenever a custom `mac.sign` hook is configured, because it cannot see
the entitlements the hook will apply. It is a **false positive** and is a
candidate for an upstream report against `electron-userland/electron-builder`.

## 5. Failure B — release blocked by a self-hosting evidence violation

### Symptom

CI/CD Pipeline run 29723467001, job *Auto Release*, step *Collect changelog and
bump version*:

```
Final release version: 0.301.0
Error recording self-hosting release metric: no committed Formal-AI-Evidence in
10e65ae2a22f206589ecba9974c95151e0124bc3 records session issue-804-claude-20260720
##[error]Process completed with exit code 1
```

### Root cause

`CONTRIBUTING.md` (lines 94–106) requires that a commit carrying
`Formal-AI-Session: <id>` also carry `Formal-AI-Evidence: <path>` pointing at a
committed file containing both `formal-ai` and *the exact session id*.

Commit `10e65ae2` declares:

```
Formal-AI-Session: issue-804-claude-20260720
Formal-AI-Evidence: dev/log/issues/804/pulls/805/analysis.md
```

but that file, as committed, contains neither the session id nor the string
`formal-ai`:

```console
$ git show 10e65ae2:dev/log/issues/804/pulls/805/analysis.md | grep -niE 'session|issue-804'
$ # (no output)
```

Reproduced exactly, from the release code path:

```console
$ rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --since 10e65ae2~1 --until 10e65ae2
self-hosting metric error: no committed Formal-AI-Evidence in 10e65ae2… records session issue-804-claude-20260720
exit=1
```

The check itself is correct and fail-closed. The **process** defect is where it
runs: `scripts/version-and-commit.rs:635-641` invokes it during Auto Release, on
`main`, *after* merge. A malformed trailer therefore cannot be caught by the
author, and it takes the entire release down. This is precisely the class of
problem R2 describes.

### Fix

A new `evidence-check` job in `.github/workflows/release.yml` runs the identical
code path over the pull request's own commits
(`git merge-base origin/$base HEAD` … `HEAD`). No logic is duplicated — the same
`scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs` measurement that gates the release now gates
the PR, so the release-time gate becomes a backstop rather than the first
detection point.

## 6. Checks that still cannot gate a pull request (R2 backlog)

Derived from a full reading of both workflows. Each item runs only on
push-to-`main`, `release`, `workflow_run` or `workflow_dispatch`:

1. Docker image build + `scripts/verify-docker-runtime.sh` (`release.yml`
   `auto-release` / `manual-release` only).
2. crates.io publish path (`publish-crate.rs`, `wait-for-crate.rs`,
   `smoke-test-published-crate.sh`). Partially covered on PRs by `build`'s
   `cargo package --list` + crate-size check.
3. `deploy-pages` — Pages artifact assembly, `stamp-pages-artifact.sh`,
   `cargo doc` for `/docs/api`.
4. `test-e2e-pages` — the whole GitHub-Pages Playwright suite is post-deploy.
5. `desktop-release.yml` `build` — **the very job that is red today**. Desktop
   packaging and macOS signing are only exercised after a release exists.
6. `desktop-release.yml` `vscode` (`.vsix` packaging) and `finalize`
   (SHA256SUMS + provenance).
7. `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh` release-resolution logic.
8. Version-bump machinery (`version-and-commit.rs`, `get-bump-type.rs`,
   `collect-changelog.rs`, `create-github-release.rs`).

Item 5 is the highest-value gap: had `desktop-release.yml`'s macOS build run on
pull requests, Failure A would have been caught in the PR that introduced the
bundled browser runtime instead of in a release.

## 7. Gaps versus the templates and CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md

Compared against `link-foundation/{rust,js,python}-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`
and `link-assistant/hive-mind/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md`:

| Practice | formal-ai | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `detect-changes` gating job | present | `release.yml:62` |
| rustfmt / clippy with `-Dwarnings` | present | `release.yml:51` |
| changelog-fragment gate | present | `release.yml:93` |
| Fresh-merge simulation (`simulate-fresh-merge.sh`) | **absent** | js template calls it in three jobs; best-practices §7 requires it |
| Secrets scan (secretlint / trufflehog) | **absent** | js template only; best-practices §11 |
| Dedicated fast `cargo-lock` job | inline in `lint` | rust template has a separate job others depend on |
| Documentation/link validation | partial | `cargo doc -D warnings` only; no link checker |
| `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient` composite | **absent** | raw `docker/setup-buildx-action@v4` |
| `.github/dependabot.yml` | **absent** | — |
| `!cancelled()` rather than `always() && !cancelled()` | not applied | best-practices §10 |

## 8. Defects to report upstream against the templates (R4)

* **T1 — no Docker/artifact PR gate** (rust template): Docker images are built
  and pushed only inside `auto-release`/`manual-release`, in the *same job* that
  runs `cargo publish`. A broken `Dockerfile` is discovered after the crate is
  already published. Workaround/fix: a `docker-pr-check` job with
  `push: false, load: true` on pull requests.
* **T2 — `simulate-fresh-merge` missing from the rust and python templates**
  although `CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §7 declares it mandatory and the js
  template ships it. The script is language-agnostic bash and can be copied
  verbatim.
* **T3 — no secrets scan in the rust and python templates**
  (best-practices §11); only the js template runs `secretlint`.
* **T4 — `CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §10 contradicts all three templates.** The
  document says to use `!cancelled()` so cancellation propagates; every template
  uses `always() && !cancelled()`, and downstream repositories (including this
  one) have copied that verbatim together with a comment asserting `always()` is
  required. Either the templates or the document must change.
* **T5 — electron-builder false-positive warning** (upstream
  `electron-userland/electron-builder`): with a custom `mac.sign` hook,
  electron-builder warns that `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`
  is required even when the configured entitlements file already grants it.

## 9. Reusable components considered

* `@electron/osx-sign` `ignore` predicate and electron-builder `mac.signIgnore`
  — used, rather than hand-rolling a codesign walker. `signIgnore` is the
  upstream-supported way to exclude a pre-signed third-party bundle and is
  already how electron-builder excludes `node_modules/playwright/.local-browsers`
  (`MacTargetHelper.js:100-102`).
* `scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs` — reused as-is for the PR gate instead of
  writing a second trailer validator, so the PR check and the release check can
  never drift apart.
* `secretlint`, `lychee` (link checking) and `simulate-fresh-merge.sh` from the
  templates are the recommended off-the-shelf answers to the §7 gaps.

## 10. Changes in this pull request

1. `desktop/package.json` — `build.mac.signIgnore` excludes
   `/Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/` from code signing, independently of
   the custom sign hook.
2. `desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` — diagnostics moved to `stderr`; one
   unconditional hook-entry banner; verbose per-file trace unchanged and still
   default-off behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG`.
3. `.github/workflows/release.yml` — new `evidence-check` job validating
   `Formal-AI-Session` / `Formal-AI-Evidence` trailers at pull-request time.
4. `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/` — raw CI logs and this analysis.

## 3. Requirements extracted from the issue

| # | Requirement | Source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R1 | Fix every false positive, false negative, warning and error in CI/CD | issue body |
| R2 | Every check must run at the **pull request** stage, not only on `main`, so each PR can produce a working release | issue comment 5020083365 |
| R3 | Compare every workflow/CI file against the three `link-foundation/*-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` repos and adopt their best practices | issue body |
| R4 | Report upstream issues against the templates when the same defect exists there | issue body |
| R5 | Follow `link-assistant/hive-mind` `docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` | issue body |
| R6 | Collect logs and evidence into `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809` | task brief |

## 4. Failure A — macOS ad-hoc codesign

### Symptom

Both `Build macos-x64` and `Build macos-arm64` fail in *Package desktop app
(macOS ad-hoc)*:

```
.../formal-ai Desktop.app/Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/Frameworks/Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework:
  replacing existing signature
  unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework
⨯ Command failed: codesign --sign - --force --timestamp=none --options runtime \
  --entitlements build/entitlements.mac.plist .../Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework
```

`Publish SHA256SUMS.txt + provenance` then fails closed with
`No artifacts from: macos-x64 macos-arm64`. That second failure is **correct
behaviour** (added deliberately in `1b0ed3f8`, "stop desktop-release from
reporting partial builds as complete") — it is a true negative, not a defect.

### Root cause

`desktop/package.json` ships Playwright's Chrome-for-Testing tree as
`extraResources` → `Contents/Resources/browser-runtime`. That tree contains
`Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework`, whose layout is **not** a
sealed macOS framework bundle: files sit directly in the framework root rather
than under `Versions/A`. `codesign` refuses such a bundle with *"unsealed
contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework"*. It is signed
upstream by Google and must be treated as an opaque resource.

`desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` already contains an exclusion
(`isBundledBrowserRuntime`, added in `46aa1dd7`) and its unit tests pass
locally. It nevertheless did not take effect in CI.

Decisive observation from `ci-logs/run-29724500254-full.log`:

* the step's env block shows `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG: 1` (workflow line 232);
* electron-builder logs `• executing custom sign  file=release/mac/formal-ai Desktop.app`;
* yet `grep -c "\[adhoc-sign-mac\]"` over the **complete** run log returns `0`,
  and `grep -c "Skipped\.\.\."` (electron-osx-sign's log line for an ignored
  path) also returns `0`.

So the exclusion never ran, even though the hook was resolved. The failing
`codesign` invocation has electron-osx-sign's own argument order
(`--sign - --force …`), i.e. it comes from `signApplication()`'s child loop in
`@electron/osx-sign`, which consults `opts.ignore` — the very list the hook was
supposed to extend.

Two things were therefore wrong, and both are fixed:

1. **The exclusion depended on a single, unverified layer.** It lived only in
   the custom `mac.sign` hook. If the hook does not run (or runs with an
   `app` path that does not resolve as expected), nothing excludes the browser
   runtime. Fix: declare the exclusion in electron-builder configuration itself,
   as `build.mac.signIgnore`. `app-builder-lib`'s
   `MacTargetHelper.buildSignOptions()` compiles `signIgnore` entries into
   regexes and folds them into the `ignore` predicate handed to
   `@electron/osx-sign` — **independently of whether a custom sign hook is
   used**. Verified against the installed `app-builder-lib@26.15.3`
   (`out/mac/MacTargetHelper.js:53-107`, option declared at
   `out/options/macOptions.d.ts:163`).
2. **The diagnostics were unobservable.** The debug logger wrote to
   `process.stdout`; electron-builder's logger and electron-osx-sign's `debug`
   both write to `stderr`. Fix: route `[adhoc-sign-mac]` output to `stderr`, and
   emit exactly one unconditional line when the hook is entered, so the next run
   answers "did the hook execute at all?" without guesswork. The detailed
   per-file trace stays behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG`, **default off**.

### Related warning (electron-builder false positive)

```
• ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the
  com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement …
```

`desktop/build/entitlements.mac.plist` already grants
`com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`. electron-builder emits this
warning whenever a custom `mac.sign` hook is configured, because it cannot see
the entitlements the hook will apply. It is a **false positive** and is a
candidate for an upstream report against `electron-userland/electron-builder`.

## 5. Failure B — release blocked by a self-hosting evidence violation

### Symptom

CI/CD Pipeline run 29723467001, job *Auto Release*, step *Collect changelog and
bump version*:

```
Final release version: 0.301.0
Error recording self-hosting release metric: no committed Formal-AI-Evidence in
10e65ae2a22f206589ecba9974c95151e0124bc3 records session issue-804-claude-20260720
##[error]Process completed with exit code 1
```

### Root cause

`CONTRIBUTING.md` (lines 94–106) requires that a commit carrying
`Formal-AI-Session: <id>` also carry `Formal-AI-Evidence: <path>` pointing at a
committed file containing both `formal-ai` and *the exact session id*.

Commit `10e65ae2` declares:

```
Formal-AI-Session: issue-804-claude-20260720
Formal-AI-Evidence: dev/log/issues/804/pulls/805/analysis.md
```

but that file, as committed, contains neither the session id nor the string
`formal-ai`:

```console
$ git show 10e65ae2:dev/log/issues/804/pulls/805/analysis.md | grep -niE 'session|issue-804'
$ # (no output)
```

Reproduced exactly, from the release code path:

```console
$ rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --since 10e65ae2~1 --until 10e65ae2
self-hosting metric error: no committed Formal-AI-Evidence in 10e65ae2… records session issue-804-claude-20260720
exit=1
```

The check itself is correct and fail-closed. The **process** defect is where it
runs: `scripts/version-and-commit.rs:635-641` invokes it during Auto Release, on
`main`, *after* merge. A malformed trailer therefore cannot be caught by the
author, and it takes the entire release down. This is precisely the class of
problem R2 describes.

### Fix

A new `evidence-check` job in `.github/workflows/release.yml` runs the identical
code path over the pull request's own commits
(`git merge-base origin/$base HEAD` … `HEAD`). No logic is duplicated — the same
`scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs` measurement that gates the release now gates
the PR, so the release-time gate becomes a backstop rather than the first
detection point.

## 6. Checks that still cannot gate a pull request (R2 backlog)

Derived from a full reading of both workflows. Each item runs only on
push-to-`main`, `release`, `workflow_run` or `workflow_dispatch`:

1. Docker image build + `scripts/verify-docker-runtime.sh` (`release.yml`
   `auto-release` / `manual-release` only).
2. crates.io publish path (`publish-crate.rs`, `wait-for-crate.rs`,
   `smoke-test-published-crate.sh`). Partially covered on PRs by `build`'s
   `cargo package --list` + crate-size check.
3. `deploy-pages` — Pages artifact assembly, `stamp-pages-artifact.sh`,
   `cargo doc` for `/docs/api`.
4. `test-e2e-pages` — the whole GitHub-Pages Playwright suite is post-deploy.
5. `desktop-release.yml` `build` — **the very job that is red today**. Desktop
   packaging and macOS signing are only exercised after a release exists.
6. `desktop-release.yml` `vscode` (`.vsix` packaging) and `finalize`
   (SHA256SUMS + provenance).
7. `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh` release-resolution logic.
8. Version-bump machinery (`version-and-commit.rs`, `get-bump-type.rs`,
   `collect-changelog.rs`, `create-github-release.rs`).

Item 5 is the highest-value gap: had `desktop-release.yml`'s macOS build run on
pull requests, Failure A would have been caught in the PR that introduced the
bundled browser runtime instead of in a release.

## 7. Gaps versus the templates and CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md

Compared against `link-foundation/{rust,js,python}-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`
and `link-assistant/hive-mind/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md`:

| Practice | formal-ai | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `detect-changes` gating job | present | `release.yml:62` |
| rustfmt / clippy with `-Dwarnings` | present | `release.yml:51` |
| changelog-fragment gate | present | `release.yml:93` |
| Fresh-merge simulation (`simulate-fresh-merge.sh`) | **absent** | js template calls it in three jobs; best-practices §7 requires it |
| Secrets scan (secretlint / trufflehog) | **absent** | js template only; best-practices §11 |
| Dedicated fast `cargo-lock` job | inline in `lint` | rust template has a separate job others depend on |
| Documentation/link validation | partial | `cargo doc -D warnings` only; no link checker |
| `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient` composite | **absent** | raw `docker/setup-buildx-action@v4` |
| `.github/dependabot.yml` | **absent** | — |
| `!cancelled()` rather than `always() && !cancelled()` | not applied | best-practices §10 |

## 8. Defects to report upstream against the templates (R4)

* **T1 — no Docker/artifact PR gate** (rust template): Docker images are built
  and pushed only inside `auto-release`/`manual-release`, in the *same job* that
  runs `cargo publish`. A broken `Dockerfile` is discovered after the crate is
  already published. Workaround/fix: a `docker-pr-check` job with
  `push: false, load: true` on pull requests.
* **T2 — `simulate-fresh-merge` missing from the rust and python templates**
  although `CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §7 declares it mandatory and the js
  template ships it. The script is language-agnostic bash and can be copied
  verbatim.
* **T3 — no secrets scan in the rust and python templates**
  (best-practices §11); only the js template runs `secretlint`.
* **T4 — `CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §10 contradicts all three templates.** The
  document says to use `!cancelled()` so cancellation propagates; every template
  uses `always() && !cancelled()`, and downstream repositories (including this
  one) have copied that verbatim together with a comment asserting `always()` is
  required. Either the templates or the document must change.
* **T5 — electron-builder false-positive warning** (upstream
  `electron-userland/electron-builder`): with a custom `mac.sign` hook,
  electron-builder warns that `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`
  is required even when the configured entitlements file already grants it.

## 9. Reusable components considered

* `@electron/osx-sign` `ignore` predicate and electron-builder `mac.signIgnore`
  — used, rather than hand-rolling a codesign walker. `signIgnore` is the
  upstream-supported way to exclude a pre-signed third-party bundle and is
  already how electron-builder excludes `node_modules/playwright/.local-browsers`
  (`MacTargetHelper.js:100-102`).
* `scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs` — reused as-is for the PR gate instead of
  writing a second trailer validator, so the PR check and the release check can
  never drift apart.
* `secretlint`, `lychee` (link checking) and `simulate-fresh-merge.sh` from the
  templates are the recommended off-the-shelf answers to the §7 gaps.

## 10. Changes in this pull request

1. `desktop/package.json` — `build.mac.signIgnore` excludes
   `/Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/` from code signing, independently of
   the custom sign hook.
2. `desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` — diagnostics moved to `stderr`; one
   unconditional hook-entry banner; verbose per-file trace unchanged and still
   default-off behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG`.
3. `.github/workflows/release.yml` — new `evidence-check` job validating
   `Formal-AI-Session` / `Formal-AI-Evidence` trailers at pull-request time.
4. `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/` — raw CI logs and this analysis.

input.content:
# CI/CD evidence for issue #808

Excerpts from the failing default-branch runs. The complete logs are 2–4 MB
each and are not committed; re-download them with:

```bash
gh run view <run-id> --repo link-assistant/formal-ai --log > run-<run-id>-full.log
```

| File | Run | Workflow | Commit | What it shows |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `run-29723467001-auto-release.log` | [29723467001](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29723467001) | CI/CD Pipeline | `76bdb6b5` | Auto Release aborts: `no committed Formal-AI-Evidence in 10e65ae2 … records session issue-804-claude-20260720` |
| `run-29719602956-excerpt.log` | [29719602956](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29719602956) | CI/CD Pipeline | `8b5acee0` | Same failure, previous commit |
| `run-29724500254-macos-codesign-excerpt.log` | [29724500254](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29724500254) | Desktop Release | `76bdb6b5` | macOS ad-hoc signing fails on the bundled Chrome for Testing framework |
| `run-29720321919-macos-codesign-excerpt.log` | [29720321919](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29720321919) | Desktop Release | `8b5acee0` | Same failure, previous commit |

## Why the macOS excerpts are filtered the way they are

The filter keeps every `##[error]`, `⨯`, `failedTask`, `unsealed`,
`executing custom sign`, `[adhoc-sign-mac]` and `Skipped...` line, plus a sample
of the `Signing... .../browser-runtime/...` lines. Three facts follow from it:

1. **1506 paths under `Contents/Resources/browser-runtime` were signed
   individually.** The exclusion in `desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` was
   therefore not in effect.
2. **Not a single `[adhoc-sign-mac]` line appears**, even though the step sets
   `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG: 1` — the hook's diagnostics never reached the
   log. (Confirmed against the *complete* log, not just the excerpt.)
3. **Not a single `Skipped...` line appears** either — `@electron/osx-sign`
   logs that for every path its `ignore` predicate rejects.

See `../analysis.md` §4 for the resulting root cause and fix.

Issue #808. Two default-branch failures, both root-caused from the run logs
collected in dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/.

Desktop Release (runs 29720321919, 29724500254) failed signing
Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/.../Google Chrome for Testing
Framework.framework with "unsealed contents present in the root directory of an
embedded framework". The exclusion in desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs was
never in effect: 1506 browser-runtime paths were signed individually, no
"Skipped..." line appears, and no [adhoc-sign-mac] line appears even though the
step sets FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG=1. The exclusion is now also declared as
electron-builder's build.mac.signIgnore, which MacTargetHelper.buildSignOptions()
applies whether or not the custom sign hook runs, and the hook's diagnostics
moved to stderr with one unconditional entry line so the next run answers
whether it executed. The per-file trace stays default-off.

CI/CD Pipeline (runs 29719602956, 29723467001) failed Auto Release with "no
committed Formal-AI-Evidence in 10e65ae2 records session
issue-804-claude-20260720": the referenced evidence file contains neither the
session id nor "formal-ai". The invariant was only enforced at release time, on
main, after merge. A new evidence-check job runs the same
scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs measurement over the pull request's own commits,
so the release-time gate becomes a backstop instead of the first detection point.

Formal-AI-Session: issue-808-claude-20260720
Formal-AI-Evidence: dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/analysis.md

content.content:
#   * workflow_dispatch      - manual rebuild for a specific (or latest) tag.
name: Desktop Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag to build (e.g. v0.152.0). Leave blank for the latest release."
        required: false
        default: ""
  release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI/CD Pipeline"]
    types: [completed]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  # The workflow_run path (the automatic one) carries neither release.tag_name
  # nor inputs.tag, so this fell through to the always-unique run_id and never
  # actually deduped: concurrent runs for the same tag raced each other on
  # `gh release upload --clobber` and on finalize's SHA256SUMS.txt. Key on the
  # commit for that path, which is what resolve maps to a tag.
  group: desktop-release-${{ github.event.release.tag_name || github.event.inputs.tag || github.event.workflow_run.head_sha || github.run_id }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  GIT_CONFIG_COUNT: '1'
  GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0: init.defaultBranch
  GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0: main
  CARGO_NET_RETRY: '10'
  CARGO_HTTP_MULTIPLEXING: 'false'
  RUSTFLAGS: -Dwarnings

jobs:
  resolve:
    name: Resolve target release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    permissions:
      contents: read
    # For workflow_run, continue after ANY completed main-branch pipeline except
    # cancelled/skipped runs (issue #479). We intentionally do NOT gate on
    # conclusion == 'success': the auto-release publishes the GitHub release in an
    # EARLY pipeline job, so a LATER job failing (e.g. the E2E Pages probe timing
    # out) still leaves a real, asset-less release that desktop assets must heal.
    # Gating on full-pipeline green meant one unrelated late failure suppressed
    # every desktop build -> "Not available in latest release". The actual
    # decision to build is delegated to scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh, whose
    # idempotency guard skips when the resolved release already carries desktop
    # assets, so running on a non-success conclusion is safe and self-healing.
    if: >-
      github.event_name != 'workflow_run' ||
      (github.event.workflow_run.head_branch == 'main' &&
       github.event.workflow_run.conclusion != 'cancelled' &&
       github.event.workflow_run.conclusion != 'skipped')
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.pick.outputs.tag }}
      should_build: ${{ steps.pick.outputs.should_build }}
    steps:
      # Check out the repo so the resolve logic lives in a unit-tested script
      # (scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh) instead of being inlined here. The
      # script only needs to *exist*; it queries the GitHub API for everything
      # else, so checking out the default ref is sufficient.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Resolve tag and whether desktop assets are needed
        id: pick
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
          INPUT_TAG: ${{ github.event.inputs.tag }}
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_sha }}
        # Issue #479: the automated release tags a CHILD "chore: release vX.Y.Z"
        # commit whose first parent is the completed CI head SHA, so matching the
        # tag to the head SHA directly always failed and desktop assets were
        # never built. desktop-release-resolve.sh resolves the latest release
        # instead (with a defensive exact-SHA tier and an idempotency guard) and
        # emits verbose ::group:: diagnostics for future debugging.
        run: bash scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh

  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.label }}
    needs: resolve
    if: needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:

content.content:
build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.label }}
    needs: resolve
    if: needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - { os: ubuntu-latest,    ebflag: "--linux", label: "linux-x64",     binext: "" }
          - { os: ubuntu-24.04-arm, ebflag: "--linux", label: "linux-arm64",   binext: "" }
          - { os: macos-15-intel,   ebflag: "--mac",   label: "macos-x64",     binext: "" }
          - { os: macos-14,         ebflag: "--mac",   label: "macos-arm64",   binext: "" }
          - { os: windows-latest,   ebflag: "--win",   label: "windows-x64",   binext: ".exe" }
          - { os: windows-11-arm,   ebflag: "--win",   label: "windows-arm64", binext: ".exe" }
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      attestations: write
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: bash
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve.outputs.tag }}

      - name: Install Rust toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: desktop-${{ matrix.label }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock', 'Cargo.toml') }}

      - name: Build formal-ai binary (release)
        run: cargo build --release --bin formal-ai

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install desktop dependencies
        run: scripts/install-node-dependencies.sh desktop

      - name: Prepare desktop resources (web + bundled binary + version sync)
        working-directory: desktop
        run: node scripts/prepare-resources.mjs

      - name: Check macOS signing secrets
        if: matrix.ebflag == '--mac'
        id: mac_secrets
        env:
          MAC_CSC_LINK: ${{ secrets.MAC_CSC_LINK }}
          MAC_CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.MAC_CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY }}
          APPLE_API_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_ID }}
          APPLE_API_ISSUER: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_ISSUER }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          missing=()
          for name in MAC_CSC_LINK MAC_CSC_KEY_PASSWORD APPLE_API_KEY APPLE_API_KEY_ID APPLE_API_ISSUER; do
            if [ -z "${!name}" ]; then
              missing+=("$name")
            fi
          done

          if [ "${#missing[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "has_secrets=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "mode=adhoc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::notice::macOS signing/notarization secrets are not configured: ${missing[*]}"
            echo "Building ad-hoc signed macOS artifacts without notarization for direct testing."
          else
            echo "has_secrets=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "mode=signed" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Package desktop app (Linux/Windows)
        if: matrix.ebflag != '--mac'
        working-directory: desktop
        env:
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: "false"
        run: npx --no-install electron-builder ${{ matrix.ebflag }} --publish never

      - name: Package desktop app (macOS signed)
        if: matrix.ebflag == '--mac' && steps.mac_secrets.outputs.has_secrets == 'true'
        working-directory: desktop

input.command: cd /tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784535043401; sed -n '250,460p' .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml

content.content:
case "${{ matrix.label }}" in
            macos-x64) ARCH="x64" ;;
            macos-arm64) ARCH="arm64" ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::Unexpected macOS matrix label: ${{ matrix.label }}"
              exit 1
              ;;
          esac

          required_macos_artifacts=(
            "release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.dmg"
            "release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.zip"
          )
          for artifact in "${required_macos_artifacts[@]}"; do
            if [ ! -f "$artifact" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$artifact::Expected versioned macOS release artifact was not created."
              ls -la release || true
              exit 1
            fi
          done

          for dmg in "release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.dmg"; do
            echo "Mounting DMG: $dmg"
            mount_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
            installed_app="$RUNNER_TEMP/formal-ai Desktop.app"

            hdiutil attach "$dmg" -mountpoint "$mount_dir" -nobrowse -readonly
            mounted_app="$mount_dir/formal-ai Desktop.app"

            if [ ! -d "$mounted_app" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Mounted DMG does not contain formal-ai Desktop.app."
              find "$mount_dir" -maxdepth 2 -print
              hdiutil detach "$mount_dir"
              exit 1
            fi

            rm -rf "$installed_app"
            ditto "$mounted_app" "$installed_app"
            hdiutil detach "$mount_dir"

            app_info="$installed_app/Contents/Info.plist"
            executable="$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :CFBundleExecutable' "$app_info")"
            bundle_id="$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :CFBundleIdentifier' "$app_info")"

            if [ "$bundle_id" != "ai.formal.desktop" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Unexpected macOS app bundle identifier: $bundle_id"
              exit 1
            fi

            if [ ! -x "$installed_app/Contents/MacOS/$executable" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Mounted app executable is missing or not executable."
              find "$installed_app/Contents" -maxdepth 3 -print
              exit 1
            fi

            if [ ! -f "$installed_app/Contents/_CodeSignature/CodeResources" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Mounted app is missing its signed CodeResources envelope."
              find "$installed_app/Contents/_CodeSignature" -maxdepth 1 -print 2>/dev/null || true
              exit 1
            fi

            codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=2 "$installed_app"

            if [ "$MACOS_BUILD_MODE" = "signed" ]; then
              spctl --assess --type execute --verbose=4 "$installed_app"
              xcrun stapler validate "$installed_app"
            else
              codesign --display --verbose=2 "$installed_app" 2>&1 | tee "$RUNNER_TEMP/formal-ai-macos-codesign.txt"
              if ! grep -Fq 'Signature=adhoc' "$RUNNER_TEMP/formal-ai-macos-codesign.txt"; then
                echo "::error file=$dmg::Expected ad-hoc macOS signature for no-Apple-account build."
                exit 1
              fi
            fi
          done

      # The macOS smoke test above is the only artifact verification this
      # workflow had, so the four Linux/Windows targets were uploaded to the
      # release with nothing checking that electron-builder actually produced
      # them under the expected names. A rename or a partial package silently
      # shipped. The deep checks are macOS-only by nature (hdiutil, codesign),
      # but existence and non-emptiness are checkable everywhere.
      - name: Smoke test Linux/Windows release artifacts
        if: matrix.ebflag != '--mac'
        shell: bash
        working-directory: desktop
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          VERSION="$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")"
          case "${{ matrix.label }}" in
            linux-x64|linux-arm64)
              ARCH="${{ matrix.label }}"; ARCH="${ARCH#linux-}"
              required=(
                "release/formal-ai-desktop-linux-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.AppImage"
                "release/formal-ai-desktop-linux-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.deb"
                "release/formal-ai-desktop-linux-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.tar.gz"
              )
              ;;
            windows-x64|windows-arm64)
              ARCH="${{ matrix.label }}"; ARCH="${ARCH#windows-}"
              required=(
                "release/formal-ai-desktop-windows-installer-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.exe"
                "release/formal-ai-desktop-windows-portable-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.exe"
              )
              ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::Unexpected matrix label for the Linux/Windows smoke test: ${{ matrix.label }}"
              exit 1
              ;;
          esac

          failed=0
          for artifact in "${required[@]}"; do
            if [ ! -f "$artifact" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$artifact::Expected versioned release artifact was not created."
              failed=1
            elif [ ! -s "$artifact" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$artifact::Release artifact is empty."
              failed=1
            else
              echo "ok $artifact ($(wc -c < "$artifact") bytes)"
            fi
          done
          if [ "$failed" -ne 0 ]; then
            ls -la release || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Collect artifacts and checksums
        id: collect
        working-directory: desktop
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          files=()
          for f in release/formal-ai-desktop-*; do
            case "$f" in
              *.dmg|*.zip|*.exe|*.AppImage|*.deb|*.tar.gz|*.blockmap) files+=("$f") ;;
            esac
          done
          for f in release/latest.yml release/latest-mac.yml release/latest-linux.yml; do
            [ -f "$f" ] && files+=("$f")
          done
          if [ ${#files[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No desktop artifacts produced for ${{ matrix.label }}" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          printf 'Artifacts:\n'; printf '  %s\n' "${files[@]}"
          # Standard `sha256sum`-format lines with bare filenames so the
          # in-browser verifier on the /download page can match by name.
          node -e '
            const fs = require("fs"); const cp = require("crypto"); const path = require("path");
            const dir = "release";
            const re = /^(formal-ai-desktop-.*\.(dmg|zip|exe|AppImage|deb|tar\.gz|blockmap)|latest(-mac|-linux)?\.yml)$/;
            const names = fs.readdirSync(dir).filter((n) => re.test(n)).sort();
            for (const n of names) {
              const hash = cp.createHash("sha256").update(fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir, n))).digest("hex");
              process.stdout.write(hash + "  " + n + "\n");
            }
          ' > "release/SHA256SUMS-${{ matrix.label }}.partial"
          cat "release/SHA256SUMS-${{ matrix.label }}.partial"

      # Attest before publishing, not after: uploading first left the assets
      # downloadable for the length of the attest step, and permanently so if
      # attest failed, with `gh attestation verify` unable to vouch for them.
      - name: Attest build provenance
        uses: actions/attest@v4
        with:
          # The released v4 checksum parser splits with the runner's native EOL,
          # so it treats our intentionally portable LF manifest as one record on
          # Windows. Digest the artifacts directly; keep the checksum fragment
          # solely for the consolidated, user-facing SHA256SUMS.txt.
          subject-path: |
            desktop/release/formal-ai-desktop-*
            desktop/release/latest*.yml

      - name: Upload assets to release
        working-directory: desktop
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          TAG: ${{ needs.resolve.outputs.tag }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          files=()
          for f in release/formal-ai-desktop-*; do
            case "$f" in
              *.dmg|*.zip|*.exe|*.AppImage|*.deb|*.tar.gz|*.blockmap) files+=("$f") ;;
            esac
          done
          for f in release/latest.yml release/latest-mac.yml release/latest-linux.yml; do
            [ -f "$f" ] && files+=("$f")
          done
          gh release upload "$TAG" "${files[@]}" --repo "$REPO" --clobber

      - name: Upload checksum fragment
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: checksums-${{ matrix.label }}
          path: desktop/release/SHA256SUMS-${{ matrix.label }}.partial
          retention-days: 7

  vscode:
    name: Package VS Code extension (.vsix)
    needs: resolve
    if: needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      attestations: write

input.old_string:
release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI/CD Pipeline"]
    types: [completed]
input.new_string:
release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI/CD Pipeline"]
    types: [completed]
  # Issue #808: packaging and macOS signing used to run only after a release
  # existed, so a desktop regression was always discovered post-merge -- which is
  # exactly how the "unsealed contents present in the root directory of an
  # embedded framework" codesign failure reached main. On pull requests the same
  # build matrix runs in dry-run mode: everything up to and including the smoke
  # tests, with every publishing step (attest, release upload, SHA256SUMS)
  # skipped. Path-filtered so unrelated pull requests do not pay for six runners.
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
    paths:
      - 'desktop/**'
      - 'editors/**'
      - '.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml'
      - 'scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh'
      - 'scripts/install-node-dependencies.sh'
