1	# Issue #808 — CI/CD false positives, false negatives, warnings and errors
2	
3	Session: `issue-808-claude-20260720`
4	Repository: formal-ai (`link-assistant/formal-ai`)
5	Pull request: [#809](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/809)
6	Evidence collected in: `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/`
7	
8	## 1. Evidence collected
9	
10	| File | Content |
11	| --- | --- |
12	| `ci-logs/run-29723467001-failed.log` | CI/CD Pipeline, `main` @ `76bdb6b5`, failed |
13	| `ci-logs/run-29724500254-failed.log` | Desktop Release, `main` @ `76bdb6b5`, failed |
14	| `ci-logs/run-29724500254-full.log` | Same run, complete log (used to prove an *absence* of output) |
15	| `ci-logs/run-29720321919-failed.log` | Desktop Release, `main` @ `8b5acee0`, failed (same signature) |
16	| `ci-logs/run-29719602956-failed.log` | CI/CD Pipeline, `main` @ `8b5acee0`, failed |
17	| `ci-logs/run-*.json` | Run/job metadata |
18	
19	## 2. Timeline
20	
21	| Time (UTC, 2026-07-20) | Event |
22	| --- | --- |
23	| 05:38 | CI/CD Pipeline run 29719602956 fails on `8b5acee0` |
24	| 05:56 | Desktop Release run 29720321919 fails on `8b5acee0` (macOS x64 + arm64) |
25	| 06:20–07:02 | Five Desktop Release runs conclude `skipped` (gated on `workflow_run` from a non-successful CI/CD Pipeline) |
26	| 07:04 | CI/CD Pipeline run 29723467001 fails on `76bdb6b5` — Auto Release, "Collect changelog and bump version" |
27	| 07:24 | Desktop Release run 29724500254 fails on `76bdb6b5` — identical macOS codesign failure |
28	| 07:51 | Desktop Release run 29725916384 concludes `skipped` |
29	| 08:10 | Issue comment: *"All checks must be in Pull Request stage, not only on main."* |
30	
31	The `skipped` runs are **not** a defect: `desktop-release.yml` triggers on
32	`workflow_run` from "CI/CD Pipeline" and its `resolve` job correctly refuses to
33	build when the upstream run did not succeed. They are downstream symptoms of the
34	two real failures below.
35	
36	## 3. Requirements extracted from the issue
37	
38	| # | Requirement | Source |
39	| --- | --- | --- |
40	| R1 | Fix every false positive, false negative, warning and error in CI/CD | issue body |
41	| R2 | Every check must run at the **pull request** stage, not only on `main`, so each PR can produce a working release | issue comment 5020083365 |
42	| R3 | Compare every workflow/CI file against the three `link-foundation/*-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` repos and adopt their best practices | issue body |
43	| R4 | Report upstream issues against the templates when the same defect exists there | issue body |
44	| R5 | Follow `link-assistant/hive-mind` `docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` | issue body |
45	| R6 | Collect logs and evidence into `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809` | task brief |
46	
47	## 4. Failure A — macOS ad-hoc codesign
48	
49	### Symptom
50	
51	Both `Build macos-x64` and `Build macos-arm64` fail in *Package desktop app
52	(macOS ad-hoc)*:
53	
54	```
55	.../formal-ai Desktop.app/Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/Frameworks/Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework:
56	  replacing existing signature
57	  unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework
58	⨯ Command failed: codesign --sign - --force --timestamp=none --options runtime \
59	  --entitlements build/entitlements.mac.plist .../Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework
60	```
61	
62	`Publish SHA256SUMS.txt + provenance` then fails closed with
63	`No artifacts from: macos-x64 macos-arm64`. That second failure is **correct
64	behaviour** (added deliberately in `1b0ed3f8`, "stop desktop-release from
65	reporting partial builds as complete") — it is a true negative, not a defect.
66	
67	### Root cause
68	
69	`desktop/package.json` ships Playwright's Chrome-for-Testing tree as
70	`extraResources` → `Contents/Resources/browser-runtime`. That tree contains
71	`Google Chrome for Testing Framework.framework`, whose layout is **not** a
72	sealed macOS framework bundle: files sit directly in the framework root rather
73	than under `Versions/A`. `codesign` refuses such a bundle with *"unsealed
74	contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework"*. It is signed
75	upstream by Google and must be treated as an opaque resource.
76	
77	`desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` already contains an exclusion
78	(`isBundledBrowserRuntime`, added in `46aa1dd7`) and its unit tests pass
79	locally. It nevertheless did not take effect in CI.
80	
81	Decisive observation from `ci-logs/run-29724500254-full.log`:
82	
83	* the step's env block shows `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG: 1` (workflow line 232);
84	* electron-builder logs `• executing custom sign  file=release/mac/formal-ai Desktop.app`;
85	* yet `grep -c "\[adhoc-sign-mac\]"` over the **complete** run log returns `0`,
86	  and `grep -c "Skipped\.\.\."` (electron-osx-sign's log line for an ignored
87	  path) also returns `0`.
88	
89	So the exclusion never ran, even though the hook was resolved. The failing
90	`codesign` invocation has electron-osx-sign's own argument order
91	(`--sign - --force …`), i.e. it comes from `signApplication()`'s child loop in
92	`@electron/osx-sign`, which consults `opts.ignore` — the very list the hook was
93	supposed to extend.
94	
95	Two things were therefore wrong, and both are fixed:
96	
97	1. **The exclusion depended on a single, unverified layer.** It lived only in
98	   the custom `mac.sign` hook. If the hook does not run (or runs with an
99	   `app` path that does not resolve as expected), nothing excludes the browser
100	   runtime. Fix: declare the exclusion in electron-builder configuration itself,
101	   as `build.mac.signIgnore`. `app-builder-lib`'s
102	   `MacTargetHelper.buildSignOptions()` compiles `signIgnore` entries into
103	   regexes and folds them into the `ignore` predicate handed to
104	   `@electron/osx-sign` — **independently of whether a custom sign hook is
105	   used**. Verified against the installed `app-builder-lib@26.15.3`
106	   (`out/mac/MacTargetHelper.js:53-107`, option declared at
107	   `out/options/macOptions.d.ts:163`).
108	2. **The diagnostics were unobservable.** The debug logger wrote to
109	   `process.stdout`; electron-builder's logger and electron-osx-sign's `debug`
110	   both write to `stderr`. Fix: route `[adhoc-sign-mac]` output to `stderr`, and
111	   emit exactly one unconditional line when the hook is entered, so the next run
112	   answers "did the hook execute at all?" without guesswork. The detailed
113	   per-file trace stays behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG`, **default off**.
114	
115	### Related warning (electron-builder false positive)
116	
117	```
118	• ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the
119	  com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement …
120	```
121	
122	`desktop/build/entitlements.mac.plist` already grants
123	`com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`. electron-builder emits this
124	warning whenever a custom `mac.sign` hook is configured, because it cannot see
125	the entitlements the hook will apply. It is a **false positive** and is a
126	candidate for an upstream report against `electron-userland/electron-builder`.
127	
128	## 5. Failure B — release blocked by a self-hosting evidence violation
129	
130	### Symptom
131

input.command:
cat > /tmp/tpl/rust-issue.md <<'EOF'
Found while auditing a downstream consumer of this template (`link-assistant/formal-ai`, issue [#808](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808)). Three of the defects we hit are present in this template itself, so downstream repos inherit them.

## 1. `always() && !cancelled()` is self-contradictory (7 occurrences)

```console
$ gh api repos/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/contents/.github/workflows/release.yml \
    -q .content | base64 -d | grep -c 'always() && !cancelled()'
7
```

`always()` is documented to make a job/step run **even if the workflow run is cancelled**. `!cancelled()` is there to express the opposite. When both appear in one expression the `always()` wins for the cancellation case, so `!cancelled()` is dead weight that reads as if it were doing something — a maintenance trap: anyone reading the guard believes cancelling the run stops the job, and it does not.

`link-assistant/hive-mind`'s `docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §10 prescribes `!cancelled()` alone, and this template's **js** sibling already does exactly that (0 occurrences of the combined form; see `js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/.github/workflows/release.yml:557`).

**Fix:** replace `always() && !cancelled()` with `!cancelled()`. One caveat we hit downstream: a single-line `if: !cancelled() && …` is invalid YAML, because a leading `!` is a tag indicator. Either wrap it — `if: ${{ !cancelled() && … }}` — or use a block scalar `if: |`.

## 2. The Dockerfile is never built at pull-request stage

The image is built and pushed only inside the release jobs, i.e. *after* `cargo publish` has already run. A Dockerfile regression therefore cannot fail a pull request; it produces a half-finished release instead — crate on crates.io, no image, no GitHub Release. We hit exactly that downstream (runs 29312084458 and 29485000765).

**Fix (what we shipped downstream):** a pull-request job that builds without pushing, so it also works for fork PRs that have no registry credentials:

```yaml
  docker-build:
    name: Docker Image Build Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60      # the image compiles the crate; 30 is not enough cold
    needs: [detect-changes]
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - run: bash scripts/free-runner-disk.sh
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with:
          context: .
          push: false
          load: true
          tags: app:pr-check
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
```

**Workaround** for repos that cannot adopt the job: run `docker build .` in an existing PR job, accepting that it does not share the buildx layer cache with the release build.

## 3. No `simulate-fresh-merge.sh` and no secrets scan

Both exist in the js sibling and neither exists here:

```console
$ grep -n 'simulate-fresh-merge\|secretlint' js-.../release.yml
118:        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh
234:        run: npx --yes -p secretlint -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend secretlint "**/*"
$ grep -c 'simulate-fresh-merge\|secretlint' rust-.../release.yml
0
```

Consequence: a pull request that is green in isolation can still break `main` (semantic merge conflict), and a committed credential is never flagged. `secretlint` is language-agnostic and runs fine in a Rust repo via `npx`; `simulate-fresh-merge.sh` is pure shell.

Happy to open pull requests for any of the three if that is useful.
EOF
gh issue create --repo link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template --title "CI: contradictory always() && !cancelled() guards, no Docker PR gate, missing fresh-merge simulation and secrets scan" --body-file /tmp/tpl/rust-issue.md

content.content: https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/100

toolUseResult.stdout: https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/100

PY

input.command:
cat > /tmp/tpl/js-issue.md <<'EOF'
Found while auditing a downstream consumer of the Rust sibling of this template (`link-assistant/formal-ai`, issue [#808](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808)). This template is the best of the three on every other axis — it is the only one with `simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, `secretlint`, and clean `!cancelled()` guards — but it shares one gap.

## The Dockerfile is never built at pull-request stage

`docker-publish` (`.github/workflows/release.yml:552`) is gated on the release jobs:

```yaml
  docker-publish:
    name: Optional Docker Hub Publish
    needs: [release, instant-release]
    if: |
      !cancelled() &&
      (
        (needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true') ||
        (needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true')
      )
```

So the image is only ever built *after* the package has been published. A Dockerfile regression cannot fail a pull request; it produces a half-finished release instead — package published, no image, no image-bearing GitHub Release. We hit exactly that downstream (runs 29312084458 and 29485000765).

**Suggested fix** — a build-only pull-request job. `push: false, load: true` keeps it working for fork pull requests, which have no registry credentials, and the GHA layer cache keeps the common case off a cold build:

```yaml
  docker-build:
    name: Docker Image Build Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    needs: [detect-changes]
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with:
          context: .
          push: false
          load: true
          tags: app:pr-check
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
```

If the repo also ships a runtime-contract script baked into the image, run it here too (`docker run --rm app:pr-check <verifier>`) — that is what we did downstream, where the verifier existed but had no caller anywhere in CI.

**Workaround** without a new job: add `docker build .` to an existing pull-request job, at the cost of not sharing the buildx layer cache with the publish build.

Companion reports for the siblings, which have additional gaps this template does not: link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#100, link-foundation/python-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#35.

Happy to open a pull request for this if useful.
EOF
gh issue create --repo link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template --title "CI: Dockerfile is only built after publish, so a broken image cannot fail a pull request" --body-file /tmp/tpl/js-issue.md

cat > /tmp/tpl/eb-issue.md <<'EOF'
## Summary

When ad-hoc signing (`identity: "-"` / no signing identity available) with `hardenedRuntime` enabled, electron-builder always logs:

```
• ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement
  to prevent app launch failures due to library validation. See https://electron.build/code-signing for details.
```

It logs this **even when the configured entitlements file already grants that entitlement**. The check never reads the entitlements, so for correctly-configured projects the warning is a pure false positive — and one that trains people to ignore a warning that is genuinely useful when it applies.

Version: `app-builder-lib@26.15.3` (via `electron-builder@26.15.3`).

## Where

`src/mac/MacTargetHelper.ts` — in the compiled output, `out/mac/MacTargetHelper.js:36-41`:

```js
if (qualifier === "-") {
    if (MacTargetHelper.isHardenedRuntimeEnabledForSigning(isMas, config)) {
        log.warn(null, "ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement " +
            "to prevent app launch failures due to library validation. See https://electron.build/code-signing for details.");
    }
    ...
```

and

```js
static isHardenedRuntimeEnabledForSigning(isMas, config) {
    return isMas ? config.hardenedRuntime === true : config.hardenedRuntime !== false;
}
```

The condition is `ad-hoc && hardenedRuntime`. `config.entitlements` is not consulted anywhere in this path.

## Reproduction

Minimal `package.json`:

```json
{
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.example.app",
    "mac": {
      "identity": null,
      "hardenedRuntime": true,
      "entitlements": "build/entitlements.mac.plist",
      "entitlementsInherit": "build/entitlements.mac.plist"
    }
  }
}
```

`build/entitlements.mac.plist` — note the entitlement the warning asks for is present:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
  <dict>
    <key>com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit</key><true/>
    <key>com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory</key><true/>
    <key>com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation</key><true/>
  </dict>
</plist>
```

```console
$ npx electron-builder --mac
...
  • ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement …
```

Expected: no warning, because the entitlement is granted. Actual: warning on every build.

## Suggested fix

Parse the resolved entitlements plist and warn only if the key is absent or false. Sketch, keeping the current behaviour when the file cannot be read (warn — that is the safe default):

```ts
private static async isLibraryValidationDisabled(config: MacConfiguration, packager: MacPackager): Promise<boolean> {
  const file = config.entitlements
  if (file == null) {
    return false // the default entitlements electron-builder ships do not grant it
  }
  try {
    const parsed = parsePlist(await fs.readFile(await packager.getResource(file), "utf8"))
    return parsed["com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation"] === true
  } catch (e) {
    log.debug({ file, error: e.message }, "cannot read entitlements to validate the ad-hoc hardened-runtime warning")
    return false
  }
}
```

then:

```ts
if (MacTargetHelper.isHardenedRuntimeEnabledForSigning(isMas, config) &&
    !(await MacTargetHelper.isLibraryValidationDisabled(config, this.packager))) {
  log.warn(null, "ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires …")
}
```

`plist` is already a dependency of `app-builder-lib`, so no new one is needed. A cheaper variant, if reading the plist here is undesirable, is to demote the message to `log.info` when `config.entitlements` is set explicitly, on the grounds that the user has taken over entitlements management.

## Workaround

None that suppresses just this line — electron-builder has no per-warning filter. Downstream we document it as a known false positive next to the entitlements file so it is not mistaken for a real signing problem.

Found while debugging macOS ad-hoc signing in `link-assistant/formal-ai` (issue [#808](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808)). Happy to send the pull request if the approach looks right.
EOF

content.content:
## 8. Defects to report upstream against the templates (R4)

* **T1 — no Docker/artifact PR gate** (rust template): Docker images are built
  and pushed only inside `auto-release`/`manual-release`, in the *same job* that
  runs `cargo publish`. A broken `Dockerfile` is discovered after the crate is
  already published. Workaround/fix: a `docker-pr-check` job with
  `push: false, load: true` on pull requests.
* **T2 — `simulate-fresh-merge` missing from the rust and python templates**
  although `CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §7 declares it mandatory and the js
  template ships it. The script is language-agnostic bash and can be copied
  verbatim.
* **T3 — no secrets scan in the rust and python templates**
  (best-practices §11); only the js template runs `secretlint`.
* **T4 — `CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md` §10 contradicts all three templates.** The
  document says to use `!cancelled()` so cancellation propagates; every template
  uses `always() && !cancelled()`, and downstream repositories (including this
  one) have copied that verbatim together with a comment asserting `always()` is
  required. Either the templates or the document must change.
* **T5 — electron-builder false-positive warning** (upstream
  `electron-userland/electron-builder`): with a custom `mac.sign` hook,
  electron-builder warns that `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`
  is required even when the configured entitlements file already grants it.

## 9. Reusable components considered

| T3 | No secrets scan (best-practices §11) | rust#100, python#35 |
| T4 | Self-contradictory `always() && !cancelled()` job guards | rust#100, python#35 |
| T5 | electron-builder false-positive `disable-library-validation` warning | [electron-builder#10027](https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/issues/10027) |

Corrections to my earlier reading, made while verifying:

  hardened-runtime build, hook or not, even when the entitlement is granted —
  which is a stronger and more easily fixed claim than the one I first wrote.

T1 applies to the js template as well (its `docker-publish` job is gated on

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# simulate-fresh-merge.sh
#
# Simulates a fresh merge of the current PR branch with the latest base branch.
# This ensures CI checks run against the actual merge result, not a stale merge preview.
#
# Usage:
#   BASE_REF=main bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh
#
# Environment variables:
#   BASE_REF  The base branch to merge with (e.g. "main"). Required.
#
# Exit code 0 = merge succeeded or not needed; non-zero = merge conflict detected.
#
# See docs/case-studies/issue-23 for why this is critical.

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Synchronizing PR with latest $BASE_REF ==="
echo "This prevents stale merge preview issues (see docs/case-studies/issue-23)"
echo ""

# Configure git for merge
git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"

# Fetch the latest base branch
echo "Fetching latest $BASE_REF..."
git fetch origin "$BASE_REF"

# Get current and base branch info
CURRENT_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
BASE_SHA=$(git rev-parse "origin/$BASE_REF")

echo "Current checkout (merge preview): $CURRENT_SHA"
echo "Latest base branch ($BASE_REF): $BASE_SHA"
echo ""

# Check if base branch has new commits not in the merge preview
BEHIND_COUNT=$(git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/$BASE_REF)

if [ "$BEHIND_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "Merge preview is up-to-date with $BASE_REF. No simulation needed."
else
  echo "Base branch has $BEHIND_COUNT new commit(s) since PR was opened/synced."
  echo "Simulating fresh merge to validate actual merge result..."
  echo ""

  # Attempt to merge the latest base branch
  if git merge origin/$BASE_REF --no-edit; then
    echo ""
    echo "Fresh merge simulation successful!"
    echo "Checks will now run against the up-to-date merged state."
  else
    echo ""
    echo "::error::Merge conflict detected! PR needs to be rebased/updated before it can be merged."
    echo "The PR branch is out of sync with $BASE_REF and cannot be automatically merged."
    exit 1
  fi
fi
echo ""
=== js usage ===
    # Typical run: <10s. This job only walks tracked files and counts lines.
    timeout-minutes: 5
    needs: [detect-changes]
    if: |
      github.event_name == 'push' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.mjs-changed == 'true' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.js-changed == 'true' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' ||
      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env:
          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh

      # Enforces the 1500-line limit on JavaScript (.js/.mjs/.cjs) and
      # Markdown (.md) files plus release.yml. This is the single source
      # of truth for the line limit; validate-docs no longer re-checks it.
      - name: Check file line limits
        run: bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh

  # === VERSION CHANGE CHECK ===
  # Prohibit manual version changes in package.json - versions should only be changed by CI/CD
  version-check:
    name: Check for Manual Version Changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          # For PRs, fetch enough history to merge with base branch
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env:
          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: '24.x'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run ESLint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Check formatting
        run: npm run format:check

      - name: Check code duplication
        run: npm run check:duplication

      - name: Check for secrets
        run: npx --yes -p secretlint -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend secretlint "**/*"

  # Test matrix: 3 runtimes (Node.js, Bun, Deno) x 3 OS (Ubuntu, macOS, Windows)
  # IMPORTANT: Tests must validate the ACTUAL merge result, not a stale merge preview.
  # See docs/case-studies/issue-23 for why this is critical.
  # Fast-fail: slow test matrix only runs after fast checks pass (hive-mind principle #5)
  test:

# lint job (line ~247): add fresh-merge + secrets scan
old_lint = """    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt, clippy
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
"""
new_lint = """    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      # Issue #808 / R3: GitHub checks out refs/pull/N/merge, a merge preview
      # computed at the last sync -- commits pushed to the base branch since
      # then are NOT in it, so a pull request can be green against a base that
      # no longer exists. Adopted from the js template, which is the only one of
      # the three that ships this.
      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env:
          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh

      # Issue #808 / R5 (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11): no secrets scan
      # existed anywhere in this pipeline. secretlint is language-agnostic and
      # needs no repository configuration for the recommended preset.
      - name: Check for secrets
        run: npx --yes -p secretlint -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend secretlint "**/*"

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt, clippy
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
"""
assert s.count(old_lint) == 1
s = s.replace(old_lint, new_lint)

old_test = """    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      # Restore the cargo cache here"""
new_test = """    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          # Needed by simulate-fresh-merge.sh, which merges origin/<base>.
          fetch-depth: 0

      # The tests are the check that most needs to run against the real merge
      # result rather than a stale preview.
      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env:
          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      # Restore the cargo cache here"""
assert s.count(old_test) == 1
s = s.replace(old_test, new_test)
open(p,'w').write(s)

Compared against `link-foundation/{rust,js,python}-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`
and `link-assistant/hive-mind/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md`:

| Practice | formal-ai | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `detect-changes` gating job | present | `release.yml:62` |
| rustfmt / clippy with `-Dwarnings` | present | `release.yml:51` |
| changelog-fragment gate | present | `release.yml:93` |
| Fresh-merge simulation (`simulate-fresh-merge.sh`) | **absent** | js template calls it in three jobs; best-practices §7 requires it |
| Secrets scan (secretlint / trufflehog) | **absent** | js template only; best-practices §11 |
| Dedicated fast `cargo-lock` job | inline in `lint` | rust template has a separate job others depend on |
| Documentation/link validation | partial | `cargo doc -D warnings` only; no link checker |
| `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient` composite | **absent** | raw `docker/setup-buildx-action@v4` |
| `.github/dependabot.yml` | **absent** | — |
| `!cancelled()` rather than `always() && !cancelled()` | not applied | best-practices §10 |

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# check-secrets.sh
#
# Scans tracked files for committed credentials with secretlint
# (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11 / issue #808).
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh [extra secretlint args...]
#
# Exit code 0 = no secrets found; non-zero = secretlint reported a finding.
#
# Why the git index rather than the `secretlint "**/*"` glob the js template
# uses: that glob walks the working tree, which here includes `target/`,
# `node_modules/` and ~150 MB of committed case-study logs, and takes longer
# than the rest of the pipeline. Only tracked files can leak a secret through a
# push, so `git ls-files` is both the faster and the more precise input.
#
# Excluded: captured third-party CI/agent logs kept verbatim as evidence. They
# are reviewed when added and consist of redacted GitHub Actions output; the
# only thing rescanning them on every pull request buys is minutes.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

mapfile -d '' files < <(
  git ls-files -z |
    grep -zv -e '^docs/case-studies/.*\.log$' -e '^dev/log/.*ci-logs/.*\.log$'
)

echo "Scanning ${#files[@]} tracked files for secrets..."

printf '%s\0' "${files[@]}" |
  xargs -0 npx --yes \
    -p secretlint \
    -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend \
    secretlint "$@"

echo "No secrets found."

p='.github/workflows/release.yml'
s=open(p).read()
old = """      # Issue #808 / R5 (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11): no secrets scan
      # existed anywhere in this pipeline. secretlint is language-agnostic, so
      # it applies to a Rust repository unchanged; .secretlintrc.json selects
      # the recommended preset.
      #
      # The js template runs `secretlint "**/*"`. That does not scale here: the
      # glob walks the working tree including `target/` and 152 MB of committed
      # case-study logs, and the scan then outlives the rest of the job. Feed it
      # the git index instead -- only tracked files can leak a secret through a
      # push -- minus the captured third-party logs, which are reviewed when
      # added and consist of redacted GitHub Actions output.
      - name: Check for secrets
        run: bash scripts/check-secrets.sh

"""
assert s.count(old) == 1
s = s.replace(old, "")

anchor = "  # === VERSION CHECK - prevents manual version modification in PRs ==="
job = """  # === SECRETS SCAN ===
  # Issue #808 / R5 (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11): no secrets scan
  # existed anywhere in this pipeline. secretlint is language-agnostic, so it
  # applies to a Rust repository unchanged; .secretlintrc.json selects the
  # recommended preset.
  #
  # Its own job rather than a step in `lint`: the scan walks every tracked file
  # and is slower than clippy on this repository, and there is no reason for it
  # to delay the lint verdict. Both run in parallel on a pull request.
  secrets-scan:
    name: Secrets Scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    needs: [detect-changes]
    if: |
      !cancelled() && (
        github.event_name == 'push' ||
        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
        needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' ||
        needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' ||
        needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'
      )
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Check for secrets
        run: bash scripts/check-secrets.sh

""" + anchor
assert s.count(anchor) == 1
s = s.replace(anchor, job)
open(p,'w').write(s)

content.content:
name: 'Set up Docker Buildx (resilient)'
description: >
  Wrapper around docker/setup-buildx-action that first pre-pulls the pinned
  BuildKit image with retries and exponential backoff, and — when Docker Hub
  itself is unreachable — falls back to a pull-through registry mirror
  (mirror.gcr.io by default) before re-tagging the image to its canonical
  reference. Booting the docker-container driver otherwise pulls moby/buildkit
  straight from Docker Hub, and a transient registry timeout there fails the
  whole publish job even when nothing is wrong with the code or the build.
  Seeding the image locally first means the boot reuses the cached image
  instead of hitting the registry, and the mirror fallback keeps the seed
  working even during a full Docker Hub registry outage.
  See docs/case-studies/issue-69/README.md and the upstream investigations in
  link-foundation/box issues #97 and #100.
inputs:
  buildkit-image:
    description: 'Pinned BuildKit image used by the docker-container driver.'
    required: false
    default: 'moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1'
  registry-mirror:
    description: >
      Pull-through Docker Hub mirror host used as a fallback when the canonical
      registry (registry-1.docker.io) is unreachable. mirror.gcr.io is Google's
      public pull-through cache of Docker Hub and is served from independent
      infrastructure, so it typically stays reachable during Docker Hub
      registry outages. Set to an empty string to disable the mirror fallback.
    required: false
    default: 'mirror.gcr.io'
  verbose:
    description: >
      Set to "true" to enable shell tracing (set -x) in the pre-pull step for
      debugging. Tracing is also enabled automatically when the workflow is run
      with step debug logging (RUNNER_DEBUG=1).
    required: false
    default: 'false'
runs:
  using: 'composite'
  steps:
    - name: Pre-pull BuildKit image (retry + registry-mirror fallback)
      shell: bash
      env:
        BUILDKIT_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.buildkit-image }}
        REGISTRY_MIRROR: ${{ inputs.registry-mirror }}
        VERBOSE: ${{ inputs.verbose }}
      run: |
        set -u
        # Verbose/debug tracing: opt-in via input, or automatic when the run is
        # started with "Enable debug logging" (RUNNER_DEBUG=1). Kept off by
        # default so normal logs stay readable.
        if [ "${VERBOSE}" = "true" ] || [ "${RUNNER_DEBUG:-}" = "1" ]; then
          echo "==> Verbose tracing enabled"
          set -x
        fi

        # Pull a reference with bounded retries and exponential backoff.
        # Returns 0 on the first successful pull, non-zero if all attempts fail.
        pull_with_retries() {
          ref="$1"
          # Attempts/backoff are overridable via env so the unit test can run
          # fast; the CI defaults stay at 5 attempts / 5s exponential backoff.
          attempts="${PREPULL_ATTEMPTS:-5}"
          delay="${PREPULL_DELAY:-5}"
          for attempt in $(seq 1 "$attempts"); do
            echo "==> Pulling ${ref} (attempt ${attempt}/${attempts})..."
            if docker pull "${ref}"; then
              return 0
            fi
            if [ "$attempt" -lt "$attempts" ]; then
              echo "==> Pull failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt..."
              sleep "$delay"
              delay=$((delay * 2))
            fi
          done
          return 1
        }

        # 1) Canonical Docker Hub. The common transient-blip case recovers here.
        if pull_with_retries "${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}"; then
          echo "==> BuildKit image cached locally from canonical registry; boot will not need a registry pull"
          exit 0
        fi

        # 2) Registry-mirror fallback. When Docker Hub's registry endpoint is
        #    fully unreachable (issue #69: registry-1.docker.io timed out for
        #    longer than a normal blip), the canonical pull above cannot recover
        #    no matter how many times it retries. mirror.gcr.io is a
        #    pull-through cache of Docker Hub on independent infrastructure, so
        #    it usually still serves the image. We pull from there and re-tag to
        #    the canonical reference so the docker-container driver boot finds it
        #    locally and never touches the failing registry.
        if [ -n "${REGISTRY_MIRROR}" ]; then
          mirror_ref="${REGISTRY_MIRROR}/${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}"
          echo "==> Canonical pull failed; trying registry mirror ${mirror_ref}"
          if pull_with_retries "${mirror_ref}"; then
            docker tag "${mirror_ref}" "${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}"
            echo "==> BuildKit image cached locally via mirror and tagged as ${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}; boot will reuse it"
            exit 0
          fi
          echo "==> Mirror pull from ${REGISTRY_MIRROR} also failed"
        fi

        # Non-fatal: fall through to setup-buildx, which will attempt its own
        # pull during boot. This preserves the previous behaviour in the worst
        # case while making the common transient-failure case recover.
        echo "==> WARNING: could not pre-pull ${BUILDKIT_IMAGE} from canonical registry or mirror; letting setup-buildx try its own boot pull"

    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4
      with:
        driver-opts: image=${{ inputs.buildkit-image }}

name: 'Set up Docker Buildx (resilient)'
description: >
  Wrapper around docker/setup-buildx-action that first pre-pulls the pinned
  BuildKit image with retries and exponential backoff, and — when Docker Hub
  itself is unreachable — falls back to a pull-through registry mirror
  (mirror.gcr.io by default) before re-tagging the image to its canonical
  reference. Booting the docker-container driver otherwise pulls moby/buildkit
  straight from Docker Hub, and a transient registry timeout there fails the
  whole publish job even when nothing is wrong with the code or the build.
  Seeding the image locally first means the boot reuses the cached image
  instead of hitting the registry, and the mirror fallback keeps the seed
  working even during a full Docker Hub registry outage.
  See docs/case-studies/issue-69/README.md and the upstream investigations in
  link-foundation/box issues #97 and #100.
inputs:
  buildkit-image:
    description: 'Pinned BuildKit image used by the docker-container driver.'
    required: false
    default: 'moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1'
  registry-mirror:
    description: >
      Pull-through Docker Hub mirror host used as a fallback when the canonical
      registry (registry-1.docker.io) is unreachable. mirror.gcr.io is Google's
      public pull-through cache of Docker Hub and is served from independent
      infrastructure, so it typically stays reachable during Docker Hub
      registry outages. Set to an empty string to disable the mirror fallback.
    required: false
    default: 'mirror.gcr.io'
  verbose:
    description: >
      Set to "true" to enable shell tracing (set -x) in the pre-pull step for
      debugging. Tracing is also enabled automatically when the workflow is run
      with step debug logging (RUNNER_DEBUG=1).
    required: false
    default: 'false'
runs:
  using: 'composite'
  steps:
    - name: Pre-pull BuildKit image (retry + registry-mirror fallback)
      shell: bash
      env:
        BUILDKIT_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.buildkit-image }}
        REGISTRY_MIRROR: ${{ inputs.registry-mirror }}
        VERBOSE: ${{ inputs.verbose }}
      run: |
        set -u
        # Verbose/debug tracing: opt-in via input, or automatic when the run is
        # started with "Enable debug logging" (RUNNER_DEBUG=1). Kept off by
        # default so normal logs stay readable.
        if [ "${VERBOSE}" = "true" ] || [ "${RUNNER_DEBUG:-}" = "1" ]; then
          echo "==> Verbose tracing enabled"
          set -x
        fi

        # Pull a reference with bounded retries and exponential backoff.
        # Returns 0 on the first successful pull, non-zero if all attempts fail.
        pull_with_retries() {
          ref="$1"
          # Attempts/backoff are overridable via env so the unit test can run
          # fast; the CI defaults stay at 5 attempts / 5s exponential backoff.
          attempts="${PREPULL_ATTEMPTS:-5}"
          delay="${PREPULL_DELAY:-5}"
          for attempt in $(seq 1 "$attempts"); do
            echo "==> Pulling ${ref} (attempt ${attempt}/${attempts})..."
            if docker pull "${ref}"; then
              return 0
            fi
            if [ "$attempt" -lt "$attempts" ]; then
              echo "==> Pull failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt..."
              sleep "$delay"
              delay=$((delay * 2))
            fi
          done
          return 1
        }

        # 1) Canonical Docker Hub. The common transient-blip case recovers here.
        if pull_with_retries "${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}"; then
          echo "==> BuildKit image cached locally from canonical registry; boot will not need a registry pull"
          exit 0
        fi

        # 2) Registry-mirror fallback. When Docker Hub's registry endpoint is
        #    fully unreachable (issue #69: registry-1.docker.io timed out for
        #    longer than a normal blip), the canonical pull above cannot recover
        #    no matter how many times it retries. mirror.gcr.io is a
        #    pull-through cache of Docker Hub on independent infrastructure, so
        #    it usually still serves the image. We pull from there and re-tag to
        #    the canonical reference so the docker-container driver boot finds it
        #    locally and never touches the failing registry.
        if [ -n "${REGISTRY_MIRROR}" ]; then
          mirror_ref="${REGISTRY_MIRROR}/${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}"
          echo "==> Canonical pull failed; trying registry mirror ${mirror_ref}"
          if pull_with_retries "${mirror_ref}"; then
            docker tag "${mirror_ref}" "${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}"
            echo "==> BuildKit image cached locally via mirror and tagged as ${BUILDKIT_IMAGE}; boot will reuse it"
            exit 0
          fi
          echo "==> Mirror pull from ${REGISTRY_MIRROR} also failed"
        fi

        # Non-fatal: fall through to setup-buildx, which will attempt its own
        # pull during boot. This preserves the previous behaviour in the worst
        # case while making the common transient-failure case recover.
        echo "==> WARNING: could not pre-pull ${BUILDKIT_IMAGE} from canonical registry or mirror; letting setup-buildx try its own boot pull"

    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4
      with:
        driver-opts: image=${{ inputs.buildkit-image }}

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# check-secrets.sh
#
# Scans the files a change touches for committed credentials, using secretlint
# with the recommended preset (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11 / issue #808).
#
# Usage:
#   BASE_REF=main bash scripts/check-secrets.sh     # scan the diff vs BASE_REF
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh --all             # scan every tracked file
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh path/to/file ...  # scan specific files
#
# Exit code 0 = no secrets found; non-zero = secretlint reported a finding.
#
# Why the diff and not the whole tree
# -----------------------------------
# The js template runs `secretlint "**/*"`. Measured on this repository that
# does not finish in a usable time: the glob walks the working tree (including
# `target/` and ~150 MB of committed case-study logs) and the scan was still
# running after 25 minutes locally. A gate that cannot be relied on to finish is
# a new CI failure mode, which is the opposite of what issue #808 asks for.
#
# Scanning what the change touches keeps the gate fast and catches the case that
# matters: a credential being *introduced*. Pre-existing files are not re-scanned
# on every pull request; run `--all` (no timeout) to audit the whole tree.
#
# FORMAL_AI_SECRETS_DEBUG=1 prints the resolved file list before scanning.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

debug() {
  if [ "${FORMAL_AI_SECRETS_DEBUG:-0}" = "1" ]; then
    printf '[check-secrets] %s\n' "$1" >&2
  fi
}

# Captured third-party CI/agent logs kept verbatim as evidence. They are
# reviewed when added and consist of redacted GitHub Actions output.
exclude() {
  grep -zv -e '^docs/case-studies/.*\.log$' -e '^dev/log/.*ci-logs/.*\.log$' || true
}

files=()
if [ "${1:-}" = "--all" ]; then
  echo "Scanning every tracked file (this is slow; there is no timeout here)."
  mapfile -d '' files < <(git ls-files -z | exclude)
elif [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
  files=("$@")
elif [ -n "${BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
  base="origin/${BASE_REF}"
  git rev-parse --verify --quiet "$base" >/dev/null || base="${BASE_REF}"
  merge_base="$(git merge-base "$base" HEAD)"
  echo "Scanning files changed in ${merge_base}..HEAD"
  # -z for paths with spaces; ACMRT drops deletions, which have nothing to scan.
  mapfile -d '' files < <(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT -z "$merge_base" HEAD | exclude)
else
  echo "Scanning uncommitted and untracked changes"
  mapfile -d '' files < <(
    { git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT -z HEAD; git ls-files -z --others --exclude-standard; } | exclude
  )
fi

if [ "${#files[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No files to scan."
  exit 0
fi

echo "Scanning ${#files[@]} file(s) for secrets..."
for f in "${files[@]}"; do
  debug "$f"
done

printf '%s\0' "${files[@]}" |
  xargs -0 npx --yes \
    -p secretlint \
    -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend \
    secretlint

echo "No secrets found."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# check-secrets.sh
#
# Scans tracked files for committed credentials with secretlint
# (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11 / issue #808).
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh [extra secretlint args...]
#
# Exit code 0 = no secrets found; non-zero = secretlint reported a finding.
#
# Why the git index rather than the `secretlint "**/*"` glob the js template
# uses: that glob walks the working tree, which here includes `target/`,
# `node_modules/` and ~150 MB of committed case-study logs, and takes longer
# than the rest of the pipeline. Only tracked files can leak a secret through a
# push, so `git ls-files` is both the faster and the more precise input.
#
# Excluded: captured third-party CI/agent logs kept verbatim as evidence. They
# are reviewed when added and consist of redacted GitHub Actions output; the
# only thing rescanning them on every pull request buys is minutes.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

mapfile -d '' files < <(
  git ls-files -z |
    grep -zv -e '^docs/case-studies/.*\.log$' -e '^dev/log/.*ci-logs/.*\.log$'
)

echo "Scanning ${#files[@]} tracked files for secrets..."

printf '%s\0' "${files[@]}" |
  xargs -0 npx --yes \
    -p secretlint \
    -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend \
    secretlint "$@"

echo "No secrets found."

input.command: time BASE_REF=main bash scripts/check-secrets.sh; echo "exit=$?"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# check-secrets.sh
#
# Scans the files a change touches for committed credentials, using secretlint
# with the recommended preset (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11 / issue #808).
#
# Usage:
#   BASE_REF=main bash scripts/check-secrets.sh     # scan the diff vs BASE_REF
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh --all             # scan every tracked file
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh path/to/file ...  # scan specific files
#
# Exit code 0 = no secrets found; non-zero = secretlint reported a finding.
#
# Why the diff and not the whole tree
# -----------------------------------
# The js template runs `secretlint "**/*"`. Measured on this repository that
# does not finish in a usable time: the glob walks the working tree (including
# `target/` and ~150 MB of committed case-study logs) and the scan was still
# running after 25 minutes locally. A gate that cannot be relied on to finish is
# a new CI failure mode, which is the opposite of what issue #808 asks for.
#
# Scanning what the change touches keeps the gate fast and catches the case that
# matters: a credential being *introduced*. Pre-existing files are not re-scanned
# on every pull request; run `--all` (no timeout) to audit the whole tree.
#
# FORMAL_AI_SECRETS_DEBUG=1 prints the resolved file list before scanning.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

debug() {
  if [ "${FORMAL_AI_SECRETS_DEBUG:-0}" = "1" ]; then
    printf '[check-secrets] %s\n' "$1" >&2
  fi
}

# Captured third-party CI/agent logs kept verbatim as evidence. They are
# reviewed when added and consist of redacted GitHub Actions output.
exclude() {
  grep -zv -e '^docs/case-studies/.*\.log$' -e '^dev/log/.*ci-logs/.*\.log$' || true
}

files=()
if [ "${1:-}" = "--all" ]; then
  echo "Scanning every tracked file (this is slow; there is no timeout here)."
  mapfile -d '' files < <(git ls-files -z | exclude)
elif [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
  files=("$@")
elif [ -n "${BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
  base="origin/${BASE_REF}"
  git rev-parse --verify --quiet "$base" >/dev/null || base="${BASE_REF}"
  merge_base="$(git merge-base "$base" HEAD)"
  echo "Scanning files changed in ${merge_base}..HEAD"
  # -z for paths with spaces; ACMRT drops deletions, which have nothing to scan.
  mapfile -d '' files < <(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT -z "$merge_base" HEAD | exclude)
else
  echo "Scanning uncommitted and untracked changes"
  mapfile -d '' files < <(
    { git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT -z HEAD; git ls-files -z --others --exclude-standard; } | exclude
  )
fi

if [ "${#files[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No files to scan."
  exit 0
fi

echo "Scanning ${#files[@]} file(s) for secrets..."
for f in "${files[@]}"; do
  debug "$f"
done

printf '%s\0' "${files[@]}" |
  xargs -0 npx --yes \
    -p secretlint \
    -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend \
    secretlint

echo "No secrets found."

lines: +  # A push event's `before` SHA is all-zeroes for a new branch, and is unknown
+  # to this clone after a force-push. Fall back to the previous commit rather
+  # than failing the job on something that is not a secret problem.
+    echo "Base ref '${BASE_REF}' does not resolve here; falling back to HEAD~1"
# -z for paths with spaces; ACMRT drops deletions, which have nothing to scan.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

   The single user task brief (from the prior session, still governing): solve GitHub issue https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/808 on branch `issue-808-bf5af32dbcdd` in `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784535043401`, updating existing PR https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/809. Verbatim requirements:
   - "Download all logs and collect data related about the issue to this repository, and compile that data into the ./dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809 folder."
   - "Use the collected evidence to do a deep analysis (search online for additional facts and data), reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events, list each and every requirement from the issue, find the root cause of each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement. Also check online for known existing components/libraries that solve a similar problem or can help."
   - "If there is not enough data to find the actual root cause, add debug output and a verbose mode (if not already present) so the root cause can be found on the next iteration. Keep the default state switched off."
   - "If the issue is related to another repository/project, report issues on GitHub for that project when possible. Each report must contain reproducible examples, workarounds, and suggestions for fixing the issue in code."
   - "Double-check that the requirements are fully applied to the entire codebase: if an issue exists in multiple places, apply it in all of them. Proceed."

   Issue #808 itself: fix all false positives, false negatives, warnings and errors in CI/CD; compare all workflow/CI files against `link-foundation/{rust,js,python}-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` and reuse their best practices, reporting the same defects upstream; follow `link-assistant/hive-mind/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md`; do everything in this single PR.

   Issue comment 5020083365: "All checks must be in Pull Request stage, not only on main, so each pull request has a chance to produce actually working release. Fix it here. All requirements of all checks must be done/addressed in this pull request."

   Requirement labels used throughout: R1 fix all CI defects; R2 all checks at PR stage; R3 template comparison/adoption; R4 upstream reports; R5 CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md; R6 evidence collection.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - GitHub Actions: `pull_request`/`workflow_run`/`release` triggers, path filters, `needs` + skipped-dependency semantics, `!cancelled()` vs `always() && !cancelled()`, concurrency groups, `cancel-in-progress`, matrix builds, `${{ }}` expression escaping, and the YAML pitfall that a bare leading `!` in an inline `if:` is a tag indicator.
   - electron-builder / `app-builder-lib@26.15.3`: `mac.signIgnore`, `MacTargetHelper.buildSignOptions()`, `isHardenedRuntimeEnabledForSigning()`, ad-hoc signing (`qualifier === "-"`), hardened runtime, entitlements plists.
   - macOS codesign: "unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework" on non-versioned framework layouts (Chrome for Testing).
   - Docker: buildx `push:false, load:true` for fork-PR-safe builds, `type=gha` layer cache, DinD image + `--privileged`, `verify-formal-ai-dind` runtime contract.
   - secretlint with `@secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend`, `.secretlintrc.json`.
   - Self-hosting metric invariant: `Formal-AI-Session` + `Formal-AI-Evidence` git trailers, validated by `scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs`.
   - `changelog.d/` fragments with `bump:` frontmatter.

3. Files and Code Sections:

   COMMITTED AND PUSHED THIS SESSION:

   - `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` (MODIFIED) — the R2 headline fix:
     ```yaml
       # Issue #808: packaging and macOS signing used to run only after a release
       # existed, so a desktop regression was always discovered post-merge -- which is
       # exactly how the "unsealed contents present in the root directory of an
       # embedded framework" codesign failure reached main. On pull requests the same
       # build matrix runs in dry-run mode: everything up to and including the smoke
       # tests, with every publishing step (attest, release upload, SHA256SUMS)
       # skipped. Path-filtered so unrelated pull requests do not pay for six runners.
       pull_request:
         types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
         paths:
           - 'desktop/**'
           - 'vscode/**'
           - '.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml'
           - 'scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh'
           - 'scripts/install-node-dependencies.sh'
     ```
     concurrency:
     ```yaml
       group: desktop-release-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.event.release.tag_name || github.event.inputs.tag || github.event.workflow_run.head_sha || github.run_id }}
       cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}
     ```
     `resolve` gate (note the extra parenthesis added to close the original clause):
     ```yaml
         if: >-
           github.event_name != 'pull_request' &&
           (github.event_name != 'workflow_run' ||
           (github.event.workflow_run.head_branch == 'main' &&
            github.event.workflow_run.conclusion != 'cancelled' &&
            github.event.workflow_run.conclusion != 'skipped'))
     ```
     `build` and `vscode` gates:
     ```yaml
         if: ${{ !cancelled() && (github.event_name == 'pull_request' || needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true') }}
     ```
     both checkouts:
     ```yaml
             # On pull requests there is no resolved tag: build the PR head, which
             # is the whole point of the dry run.
             ref: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.resolve.outputs.tag || '' }}
     ```
     `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'` added to `Attest build provenance` (both jobs), `Upload assets to release`, `Upload .vsix to release`; `finalize` gate became `if: ${{ always() && github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true' }}`.

   - `.github/workflows/release.yml` (MODIFIED, committed) — new `docker-build` job inserted before `version-check`:
     ```yaml
       docker-build:
         name: Docker Image Build & Runtime Check
         runs-on: ubuntu-latest
         timeout-minutes: 60
         needs: [detect-changes]
         if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'
         steps:
           - uses: actions/checkout@v6
           - name: Free up runner disk space
             run: bash scripts/free-runner-disk.sh
           - name: Set up Docker Buildx
             uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4   # later rewired to ./.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient
           - name: Build image
             uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
             with:
               context: .
               push: false
               load: true
               tags: formal-ai:pr-check
               cache-from: type=gha
               cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
           - name: Verify image runtime contract
             run: |
               set -euo pipefail
               docker run --rm --privileged formal-ai:pr-check verify-formal-ai-dind
     ```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# check-secrets.sh
#
# Scans the files a change touches for committed credentials, using secretlint
# with the recommended preset (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 11 / issue #808).
#
# Usage:
#   BASE_REF=main bash scripts/check-secrets.sh     # scan the diff vs BASE_REF
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh --all             # scan every tracked file
#   bash scripts/check-secrets.sh path/to/file ...  # scan specific files
#
# Exit code 0 = no secrets found; non-zero = secretlint reported a finding.
#
# Why the diff and not the whole tree
# -----------------------------------
# The js template runs `secretlint "**/*"`. Measured on this repository that
# does not finish in a usable time: the glob walks the working tree (including
# `target/` and ~150 MB of committed case-study logs) and the scan was still
# running after 25 minutes locally. A gate that cannot be relied on to finish is
# a new CI failure mode, which is the opposite of what issue #808 asks for.
#
# Scanning what the change touches keeps the gate fast and catches the case that
# matters: a credential being *introduced*. Pre-existing files are not re-scanned
# on every pull request; run `--all` (no timeout) to audit the whole tree.
#
# FORMAL_AI_SECRETS_DEBUG=1 prints the resolved file list before scanning.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

debug() {
  if [ "${FORMAL_AI_SECRETS_DEBUG:-0}" = "1" ]; then
    printf '[check-secrets] %s\n' "$1" >&2
  fi
}

# Captured third-party CI/agent logs kept verbatim as evidence. They are
# reviewed when added and consist of redacted GitHub Actions output.
exclude() {
  grep -zv -e '^docs/case-studies/.*\.log$' -e '^dev/log/.*ci-logs/.*\.log$' || true
}

files=()
if [ "${1:-}" = "--all" ]; then
  echo "Scanning every tracked file (this is slow; there is no timeout here)."
  mapfile -d '' files < <(git ls-files -z | exclude)
elif [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
  files=("$@")
elif [ -n "${BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
  base="origin/${BASE_REF}"
  git rev-parse --verify --quiet "$base" >/dev/null || base="${BASE_REF}"
  # A push event's `before` SHA is all-zeroes for a new branch, and is unknown
  # to this clone after a force-push. Fall back to the previous commit rather
  # than failing the job on something that is not a secret problem.
  if ! git rev-parse --verify --quiet "${base}^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
    echo "Base ref '${BASE_REF}' does not resolve here; falling back to HEAD~1"
    base="HEAD~1"
  fi
  merge_base="$(git merge-base "$base" HEAD)"
  echo "Scanning files changed in ${merge_base}..HEAD"
  # -z for paths with spaces; ACMRT drops deletions, which have nothing to scan.
  mapfile -d '' files < <(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT -z "$merge_base" HEAD | exclude)
else
  echo "Scanning uncommitted and untracked changes"
  mapfile -d '' files < <(
    { git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT -z HEAD; git ls-files -z --others --exclude-standard; } | exclude
  )
fi

if [ "${#files[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No files to scan."
  exit 0
fi

echo "Scanning ${#files[@]} file(s) for secrets..."
for f in "${files[@]}"; do
  debug "$f"
done

printf '%s\0' "${files[@]}" |
  xargs -0 npx --yes \
    -p secretlint \
    -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend \
    secretlint

echo "No secrets found."

---
bump: patch
---

### Fixed
- Replaced the contradictory `always() && !cancelled()` job guards in the CI/CD pipeline with `!cancelled()`, so cancelling a run actually stops the dependent jobs (issue #808).
- Excluded the bundled Chrome for Testing runtime from macOS code signing via `electron-builder`'s `mac.signIgnore`, so ad-hoc desktop builds no longer fail with "unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework" (issue #808).
- Routed the macOS ad-hoc signing diagnostics to stderr and added an unconditional hook-entry line, so the sign trace actually reaches CI logs; the per-file trace stays default-off behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG` (issue #808).

### Added
- Desktop and VS Code extension packaging now run on pull requests that touch `desktop/`, `vscode/` or the release workflow, exercising the full six-target build matrix, macOS signing and the artifact smoke tests in dry-run mode with every publishing step skipped (issue #808).
- Pull-request job building the Docker image and running `scripts/verify-docker-runtime.sh` inside it, so a broken `Dockerfile` can no longer surface only after the crate has been published (issue #808).
- Pull-request job validating `Formal-AI-Session` / `Formal-AI-Evidence` commit trailers with the same measurement the release uses, so a malformed trailer is rejected before it can fail Auto Release on `main` (issue #808).

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## 10. Changes in this pull request

1. `desktop/package.json` — `build.mac.signIgnore` excludes
   `/Contents/Resources/browser-runtime/` from code signing, independently of
   the custom sign hook.
2. `desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` — diagnostics moved to `stderr`; one
   unconditional hook-entry banner; verbose per-file trace unchanged and still
   default-off behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG`.
3. `.github/workflows/release.yml` — new `evidence-check` job validating
   `Formal-AI-Session` / `Formal-AI-Evidence` trailers at pull-request time.
4. `.github/workflows/release.yml` — new `docker-build` job; replaced the
   contradictory `always() && !cancelled()` guard with plain `!cancelled()` in
   all nine places (CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md §10: `always()` makes a job run even
   when the run is cancelled, which is the opposite of the intent, and the
   `&& !cancelled()` half is dead weight that reads as if it did something).
5. `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` — path-filtered `pull_request`
   trigger running the full desktop + `.vsix` packaging matrix in dry-run mode
   (see §6); publishing steps and `finalize` stay release-only.
6. `desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.test.cjs` — regression test asserting the
   `mac.signIgnore` patterns match the browser runtime and not the app binary.

import re
p='tests/unit/ci-cd/workflow_release.rs'; s=open(p).read()
s=s.replace("""        8,
        "each rust-script install step should use the retry wrapper""","""        // +1 for the evidence-check job added for issue #808.
        9,
        "each rust-script install step should use the retry wrapper""")
s=s.replace("""        test.contains("always() && !cancelled()"),
        "test job needs always() so the skipped detect-changes dependency does \\
         not cascade on workflow_dispatch\"""","""        // Issue #808 / CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md section 10: `always()` also runs
        // the job when the *run* is cancelled, which is not what this gate wants.
        // `!cancelled()` is enough to stop a skipped `detect-changes` from
        // cascading -- any status-check function disables the auto-skip.
        test.contains("!cancelled()") && !test.contains("always()"),
        "test job needs !cancelled() so the skipped detect-changes dependency \\
         does not cascade on workflow_dispatch\"""")
s=s.replace("""        ("changelog", 10),
        ("version-check", 5),""","""        ("changelog", 10),
        // Issue #808: pull-request gates for the trailer invariant, the Docker
        // image and committed credentials.
        ("evidence-check", 10),
        ("docker-build", 60),
        ("secrets-scan", 10),
        ("version-check", 5),""")
open(p,'w').write(s)
PY
grep -rn "always() && !cancelled()" tests/ .github/ | head

input.new_string:
MACOS_ADHOC_SIGN: "1"
          # Issue #808: electron-builder's isSignAllowed() bails out with
          # "Current build is a part of pull request, code signing will be
          # skipped" whenever GITHUB_BASE_REF is set (builder-util
          # `isPullRequest()`), so the pull-request dry run produced an unsigned
          # .app with no CodeResources and the smoke test failed. The security
          # warning attached to this flag is about *secret* certificates leaking
          # to fork builds; this step only runs when no signing secrets exist and
          # signs ad-hoc with identity `-`, so there is nothing to leak.
          CSC_FOR_PULL_REQUEST: "true"
          FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG: "1"

The pull-request packaging dry run produced an unsigned .app, so the smoke
test failed with \"Mounted app is missing its signed CodeResources envelope\".
Root cause, from the run 29728829534 log: electron-builder's isSignAllowed()
logs \"Current build is a part of pull request, code signing will be skipped\"
whenever builder-util's isPullRequest() sees GITHUB_BASE_REF, so the custom
sign hook was never invoked -- which is also why its debug banner never
appeared. CSC_FOR_PULL_REQUEST=true re-enables it; the flag's documented
security concern is about secret certificates, and this step only runs when no
signing secrets are configured and signs ad-hoc with identity '-'.

Also updates the workflow-invariant tests for the jobs added for this issue and
retires the stale always() comments the guards no longer use.

content.content:
### Related warning (electron-builder false positive)

```
• ad-hoc signing with hardenedRuntime enabled requires the
  com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation entitlement …
```

`desktop/build/entitlements.mac.plist` already grants
`com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation`. electron-builder emits this
warning whenever a custom `mac.sign` hook is configured, because it cannot see
the entitlements the hook will apply. It is a **false positive** and is a
candidate for an upstream report against `electron-userland/electron-builder`.

## 5. Failure B — release blocked by a self-hosting evidence violation

python3 - <<'PY'
p='dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/analysis.md'; s=open(p).read()
s=s.replace("## 5. Failure B — release blocked by a self-hosting evidence violation","""### Second root cause, found by the added debug output (pull-request runs)

The instrumentation added in the first commit did its job. With packaging now
running on pull requests, both macOS jobs of run `29728829534` failed a *later*
check:

```
##[error]Mounted app is missing its signed CodeResources envelope.
```

and the hook's unconditional entry banner never appeared. The packaging log
explains why:

```
• Current build is a part of pull request, code signing will be skipped.
```

`app-builder-lib/out/codeSign/macCodeSign.js:28` calls builder-util's
`isPullRequest()` from `isSignAllowed()` and returns `false` before
`MacPackager.sign()` reaches `findSigningIdentity()`, so the custom
`mac.sign` hook is never invoked at all. `isPullRequest()` is true whenever
`GITHUB_BASE_REF` is set, i.e. on every `pull_request` event.

Fix: `CSC_FOR_PULL_REQUEST: "true"` on the ad-hoc packaging step only. The
security concern documented for that flag is about *secret* certificates being
usable by fork builds; this step runs only when no signing secrets are
configured and signs ad-hoc with identity `-`, so there is no secret to expose.

## 5. Failure B — release blocked by a self-hosting evidence violation""")
open(p,'w').write(s)

### Added""")
open(p,'w').write(s)

input.command:
cat > /tmp/prbody.md <<'EOF'
## Summary

Fixes the CI/CD defects behind issue #808 and moves the checks that previously
only ran on `main` into the pull-request stage, so a pull request can no longer
be green while the release it produces is broken.

Fixes #808

## Root causes found (evidence in `dev/log/issues/808/pulls/809/`)

1. **macOS codesign failure on release** — `codesign` refused the bundled Chrome
   for Testing runtime ("unsealed contents present in the root directory of an
   embedded framework"): its framework layout is not versioned. Fixed with
   `mac.signIgnore`, independently of the custom sign hook.
2. **The macOS sign hook produced no output at all** — this was unexplained, so
   the first commit added an unconditional hook-entry banner and routed the
   diagnostics to stderr. On the first pull-request packaging run that
   instrumentation answered it: `app-builder-lib`'s `isSignAllowed()` returns
   early with *"Current build is a part of pull request, code signing will be
   skipped"* whenever `GITHUB_BASE_REF` is set, so the hook is never invoked and
   the app ships without a `CodeResources` envelope. Fixed with
   `CSC_FOR_PULL_REQUEST` on the ad-hoc step only (no secrets are in play there).
3. **Auto Release blocked by a malformed self-hosting evidence trailer** — now
   validated at pull-request time by a new `evidence-check` job.

## All checks now run at the pull-request stage (issue comment 5020083365)

- Desktop + `.vsix` packaging: the full six-target matrix, macOS signing and the
  artifact smoke tests run on pull requests in **dry-run** mode — every
  publishing step (attest, release upload, `SHA256SUMS`, `finalize`) is skipped.
  Path-filtered so unrelated pull requests do not pay for six runners.
- Docker image build + `verify-formal-ai-dind` runtime contract (`push: false`,
  so it is fork-safe).
- Self-hosting evidence trailers.
- Secrets scan.
- Fresh-merge simulation against the current `main`.

## Practices adopted from the pipeline templates (R3/R5)

Compared against `link-foundation/{rust,js,python}-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`
and `hive-mind/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md`:

- `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` (verbatim from the js template) — a stale
  merge preview now fails the pull request instead of breaking `main`.
- `scripts/check-secrets.sh` + `.secretlintrc.json`. Scoped to the files a change
  touches: the template's `secretlint "**/*"` did not finish in 25 minutes on
  this tree, while the diff scan measures 2.6 s. Verified it actually fires — a
  planted `ghp_…` token is reported as `[GITHUB_TOKEN] found GitHub Token`.
- `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient` (verbatim from the rust template),
  replacing all three raw `docker/setup-buildx-action@v4` uses.
- Replaced all nine `always() && !cancelled()` guards with `!cancelled()`
  (best-practices §10): `always()` also runs the job when the run is *cancelled*,
  which is the opposite of the intent.

## Defects reported upstream (R4)

Four issues filed with reproducible examples, workarounds and code suggestions —
see §8 of the analysis for the table and for two self-corrections I made while
verifying my own earlier claims.

## Verification

- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::` — 131 passed. The workflow-invariant tests
  were updated for the new jobs rather than around them.
- `bash scripts/check-secrets.sh`, `bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` and
  `rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --since main --until HEAD` all run
  clean locally.
- Regression test asserting the `mac.signIgnore` patterns match the browser
  runtime and not the app binary.
EOF

content.text:
I'll wait for these runs to finish before doing anything further. Status so far:

**Pushed since resuming** (branch `issue-808-bf5af32dbcdd`, PR [#809](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/809)):

1. **R3/R5 adoption** — `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, `scripts/check-secrets.sh` + `.secretlintrc.json` behind a new `secrets-scan` job, and `.github/actions/setup-buildx-resilient` replacing all three raw `docker/setup-buildx-action@v4` uses.
2. **Merged the 14 new `main` commits** (only conflict was `.gitkeep` timestamps) and rewrote the two new commit messages to the session id the evidence file records — `self-hosting-metric.rs --since main --until HEAD` now reports 18.86% (7/20 commits) instead of erroring.
3. **A real root cause the added instrumentation exposed.** The pull-request packaging dry run failed with *"Mounted app is missing its signed CodeResources envelope"* and the hook banner never printed. The packaging log shows `app-builder-lib`'s `isSignAllowed()` bailing with *"Current build is a part of pull request, code signing will be skipped"* — `builder-util`'s `isPullRequest()` is true whenever `GITHUB_BASE_REF` is set, so the custom `mac.sign` hook is never invoked. Fixed with `CSC_FOR_PULL_REQUEST: "true"` on the ad-hoc step only, where no signing secrets exist and the identity is `-`, so the flag's documented secret-leak concern does not apply.
4. **Workflow-invariant tests updated for the new jobs** (`evidence-check`, `docker-build`, `secrets-scan`), and the `always() && !cancelled()` assertion replaced along with the stale comments; `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::` is 131/131 green locally.
5. Analysis §7/§10 updated — including the correction that no template ships `.github/dependabot.yml`, so that row was never a real gap — plus changelog fragments and a rewritten PR title/description.

The PR is still a draft; I'll mark it ready once the runs on `ed943da` come back green (or investigate the logs if they don't).
